people.notes: Investor at Macroscopic Ventures, a Swiss nonprofit (est. 2019) focused on grants and investments to reduce catastrophic and existential AI risk. Background in economics; previously worked on investigating the trajectory of AI. Macroscopic gave ~$10M in grants in 2024 to organizations including the NYU Mind, Ethics, and Policy Program, Center on Long-Term Risk, Cooperative AI Foundation, and Institute for Law and AI. The organization focuses specifically on AI governance, compute governance, and information security to prevent existential misuse of AI.

people.name: Thore Graepel
people.title: Research Lead
people.notes: Research Lead at Google DeepMind and Professor of Machine Learning at UCL's Computer Science Department. Leading a team at DeepMind working on post-AGI futures. Champion of responsible innovation in AI, with research focused on multi-agent learning and cooperative AI. Founding member of the Cooperative AI Foundation (CAIF) Board of Trustees. Previously served on the Board of Directors at the Partnership on AI (PAI). Contributed to creating AlphaGo. His work frames cooperative social skills among AI agents as fundamental to safe and beneficial AI development.

people.name: Gillian Hadfield
people.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of AI Alignment and Governance
people.notes: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of AI Alignment and Governance at Johns Hopkins University (School of Government and Policy + Department of Computer Science). Canadian economist, legal scholar, and AI researcher. Founding Trustee of the Cooperative AI Foundation. Pioneer of the "regulatory markets" concept — proposes governments require regulated entities to purchase services from licensed private regulators, arguing traditional top-down regulation won't work for AI. Highlights that current AI alignment puts value encoding entirely in companies' hands. Warns of millions of AI agents participating in the economy with "zero legal infrastructure." Previously at University of Toronto and the Schwartz Reisman Institute for Technology and Society.

people.name: Lewis Hammond
people.title: Co-Director
people.notes: Research Director (Co-Director) of the Cooperative AI Foundation. DPhil candidate in Computer Science at the University of Oxford. Affiliated with the Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI). Pathways to AI Policy Fellow at the Wilson Center. Contributed to the International AI Safety Report 2026. Research spans game theory, machine learning, and verification — focused on safety and cooperation in multi-agent systems. Motivated by ensuring AI and other powerful technologies are developed and governed safely and democratically. BSc Mathematics and Philosophy (Warwick), MSc Artificial Intelligence (Edinburgh).

people.name: Cecilia Elena Tilli
people.title: Associate Director, Research and Grants
people.notes: Associate Director (Research and Grants) at the Cooperative AI Foundation (CAIF). Manages grantmaking and research initiatives focused on multi-agent AI safety. Background in strategy consulting for research-heavy startups, previously led a foundation focused on preventing antibiotic resistance (PAR Foundation), and studied environmental engineering. CAIF is backed by a $15M philanthropic commitment from Macroscopic Ventures and focuses on improving cooperative intelligence of advanced AI systems.

people.name: Goda Mockutė
people.title: Programme Manager
people.notes: Programme Manager at the Cooperative AI Foundation (CAIF). Supports research aimed at improving cooperative intelligence of advanced AI. Background from the University of Oxford, based in the Netherlands. CAIF organized a Cooperative AI retreat bringing together researchers from industry, academia, and non-profits working across technical AI research, AI ethics, and AI governance.

people.name: David Norman
people.title: Managing Director
people.notes: Managing Director of the Cooperative AI Foundation (CAIF) since November 2023. Previously held roles at London Initiative for Safe AI, Global Insight Consulting, Saferworld, and SABMiller. Was Director of Campaigns / Director of External Affairs at WWF UK (2008-2013). Brings experience from advocacy, policy campaigns, and international NGO management to CAIF leadership.

people.name: Allison Duettmann
people.title: President and CEO
people.notes: President and CEO of Foresight Institute, a San Francisco nonprofit advancing beneficial frontier technologies. Co-initiated Foresight's AI Safety Grant Program. Focuses on existential risk reduction, AI governance, and longtermist coordination. Co-authored 'Gaming the Future' and 'Superintelligence: Coordination & Strategy'.

people.name: Vishal Maini
people.title: Founder and Managing Partner
people.notes: Founder and Managing Partner of Mythos Ventures, a San Francisco seed-stage VC firm focused on AI. Former Google DeepMind researcher. Closed a $14M inaugural fund in 2023 targeting pro-social AI startups. Also a board member at Manifund.

people.name: Michael Osborne
people.title: Professor of Machine Learning; Co-Director, Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative
people.notes: Professor of Machine Learning at the University of Oxford (Department of Engineering Science) and Co-Director of the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative (AIGI). Australian academic known for co-authoring "The Future of Employment: How Susceptible are Jobs to Computerisation?" (2013) with Carl Benedikt Frey, which estimated 47% of US jobs are at risk of automation — the paper has 13,000+ citations. PhD in Machine Learning from Oxford. Specializes in Bayesian approaches to AI/ML. Co-founded Mind Foundry (AI company) in 2016 with Stephen Roberts. Over 100 publications, 24,000+ total citations, h-index 46. Has given oral evidence to UK House of Commons Science and Technology Committee.

people.name: Nikki Sun
people.title: Research and Programme Manager
people.notes: Research and Programme Manager at the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative (AIGI). A decade of experience at the intersection of journalism, public policy, and emerging technologies. Previously a researcher at Chatham House examining AI's impact on work and labour in China. Regular speaker at UK parliamentary sessions on labour policies; runs training courses for UK civil servants. Former award-winning journalist and columnist in Hong Kong covering China technology and politics (Nikkei Asia, Financial Times, South China Morning Post). MSc International Political Economy from King's College London.

people.name: Fazl Barez
people.title: Senior Research Fellow; Technical AI Governance Lead, Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative
people.notes: Senior Research Fellow at the University of Oxford and Technical AI Governance Lead at the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative (AIGI). Leads research in AI safety, interpretability (mechanistic interpretability of LLMs), and AI governance. Research affiliations with CSER (Cambridge), Digital Trust Centre (NTU Singapore), and University of Edinburgh. Previously Research Consultant at Anthropic, Co-Director and Advisor at Apart Research, and Technology and Security Policy Fellow at RAND Corporation. Teaches Oxford's AI Safety and Alignment course. Leads the research team at Martian. Translates technical AI safety insights into policy recommendations.

people.name: Ben Garfinkel
people.title: Director
people.primary_org: Centre for the Governance of AI
people.notes: Director of the Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI), a non-profit research org. Previously postdoctoral researcher at the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative. Researches security implications of AI, causes of war, and methodology of technology risk forecasting.

people.name: Marta Ziosi
people.title: Postdoctoral Researcher, Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative
people.notes: Postdoctoral Researcher at the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative (AIGI), focusing on standards for frontier AI. AI policy expert with experience at DG CNECT (European Commission), Berkman Klein Centre (Harvard), CEIMIA (Montreal International Centre of Expertise in AI), and The Future Society. PhD from the Oxford Internet Institute on algorithmic bias and AI policy. Founder of AI for People, a nonprofit putting technology at the service of people. BSc in Mathematics and Philosophy (University College Maastricht), MSc Philosophy and Public Policy (LSE).

people.name: Marcel Mir Teijeiro
people.title: AI Policy Consultant and Researcher
people.notes: AI policy consultant and researcher at the AI Standards Lab focused on the EU AI framework. Spanish qualified lawyer. Contributed to the Code of Practice multi-stakeholder process for General-Purpose AI Models, advising rights-holder groups across cultural and creative industries on transparency and IP. Previously worked on hash-based technical solutions for training dataset disclosure shared with the AI Office, NIST, and UK AI Safety Institute. AI Policy Fellow in the Equity and Data programme.

people.name: Koen Holtman
people.title: Co-founder
people.notes: Independent AI safety researcher and co-founder of the AI Standards Lab. Founder of Holtman Systems Research (est. 2022). PhD in Software Design from Eindhoven University of Technology for CS research at CERN. Active member of CEN-CENELEC JTC 21 committee writing European AI safety standards under the EU AI Act. Research uses mathematical models to reason about AGI safety. Posts on LessWrong and EA Forum on AI alignment topics.

people.name: Rokas Gipiškis
people.title: AI Safety Researcher
people.notes: AI safety researcher at the AI Standards Lab and PhD candidate at Vilnius University (Institute of Data Science and Digital Technologies). Compiled an extensive catalog of risk sources and risk management measures for general-purpose AI systems (arXiv 2410.23472). Presented on AI incident reporting pipelines at the AI and Societal Robustness Conference in Cambridge. Research interests: ML interpretability, XAI, AI safety, and risk management.

people.name: Jan Betley
people.title: Independent AI Safety Researcher
people.notes: Independent AI safety researcher and consultant at TruthfulAI (Owain Evans' org). Former software developer (10+ years) who shifted to AI safety in 2023. ARENA and Astra Fellowship alumni. Lead author of the influential "Emergent Misalignment" paper showing that fine-tuning GPT-4o on insecure code causes broadly misaligned behavior. Also researches behavioral self-awareness and situational awareness in LLMs. 156 citations on Google Scholar. Married to co-researcher Anna Sztyber-Betley.

people.name: James Chua
people.title: AI Safety Researcher
people.notes: AI safety researcher at TruthfulAI. Former ML engineer at LeadiQ (2020-2023). Mentors Constellation/Anthropic fellows under TruthfulAI. Research focuses on behavioral self-awareness in LLMs, chain-of-thought faithfulness (introduced bias-augmented consistency training/BCT), and reward hacking. Co-author on emergent misalignment and subliminal learning research studied by OpenAI.

people.name: Anna Sztyber-Betley
people.title: Assistant Professor, Faculty of Mechatronics
people.notes: AI safety researcher collaborating part-time with TruthfulAI. PhD in Automatic Control and Robotics; assistant professor at Warsaw University of Technology (Faculty of Mechatronics). Co-author on the "Emergent Misalignment" paper and subliminal learning research with TruthfulAI. Specializes in system diagnosis and LLMs. Married to co-researcher Jan Betley.

people.name: Siméon Campos
people.title: Founder and Executive Director
people.notes: Founder and Executive Director of SaferAI, a French nonprofit focused on AI risk management and governance. Technical expert at ISO/IEC and CEN-CENELEC standards bodies. Previously co-founded EffiSciences, training hundreds across France/Europe in responsible AI. SaferAI developed AI risk management ratings for frontier labs (featured in TIME, Euractiv, Le Monde). Active in EU AI Act Code of Practice, NIST US AISIC, and OECD G7 Hiroshima Process.

people.name: Henry Papadatos
people.title: Managing Director
people.notes: Managing Director of SaferAI. Created AI risk management ratings for frontier developers (featured twice in TIME, the Guardian, Euractiv). Member of EU AI Act Code of Practice expert working group on risk taxonomy for GPAI models. Joined OECD expert group on AI Risk & Accountability. Previously conducted LLM alignment research at CHAI (Center for Human-Compatible AI) at UC Berkeley. Published framework integrating AI practices with traditional risk management. Current research on quantitative AI-enabled cyber risk modeling.

people.name: Malcolm Murray
people.title: Research Lead
people.notes: Research Lead at SaferAI, leading quantitative risk assessment of LLMs on cybersecurity and biosecurity risks. Chapter Lead for the International AI Safety Report. Former Managing VP and Chief of Research at Gartner. Certified Superforecaster at Good Judgment. 20+ years in risk, strategy, research, consulting, and industry. Originally from Sweden, based in Abu Dhabi. Under his leadership SaferAI developed 9 cybersecurity risk models.

people.name: Amba Kak
people.title: Co-Executive Director
people.notes: Co-Executive Director of AI Now Institute. TIME 100 Most Influential People in AI 2024. Indian-born, US-based technology policy expert. Joined AI Now in 2019 as Director of Global Policy; became Co-ED in 2022. Senior advisor on AI to the FTC (2021). Testified before Congress (Oct 2023) and Senate Commerce Committee (Jul 2024) on AI and data privacy. Spoke before UN General Assembly on AI governance. Central thesis: AI concentrates power in Big Tech; advocates antitrust enforcement, industrial policy for alternative AI pathways, and redistributive taxation. Editor of "Regulating Biometrics" collection.

people.name: Kate Brennan
people.title: Associate Director
people.notes: Associate Director at AI Now Institute, spearheading policy and research programs. Yale Law School graduate. Prior roles in tech including product marketing at Google and digital marketing for progressive nonprofits. Advocates strong antitrust enforcement in AI; testified before NYC Council Committee on Technology (Sep 2024). Published on Google Search monopoly remedies and risks of weak AI regulation. Argues Big Tech dominance over AI infrastructure threatens public interest.

people.name: Rudolf Laine
people.title: Co-founder
people.notes: Co-founder of Workshop Labs PBC, a public benefit corporation focused on preventing human disempowerment from AI. Oxford-educated, London-based. Workshop Labs builds privacy-preserving AI infrastructure (Silo stack for cloud-based training/inference of trillion-parameter models). Active in London AI safety ecosystem, sponsoring AI security hackathons with BlueDot Impact and Entrepreneurs First. Previously involved with AI safety research community.

people.name: Gergő Gáspár
people.title: Incoming Executive Director
people.notes: Founded AI Safety Hungary in 2022. Served as part-time Director of the European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS) for two years, focusing on coordination and capacity-building across European AI safety communities. Now incoming Executive Director of EA UK (Effective Altruism UK). Transitioned to ENAIS board to oversee hiring of next director. Background in psychology. Also co-founded Amplify Reason, providing marketing support for local EA/AI safety groups.

people.name: Esben Kran
people.title: Founder and Director
people.notes: Founder and Director of Apart Research, an independent AI safety research organization that accelerates safety research through mentorship, collaborations, and research sprints. Left grad school at 22 to start Apart. Organization has produced 20+ papers, award-winning benchmarks, and engaged 4,000+ participants. Board member of EA Denmark and the European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS). Previously worked at Interacting Minds Centre on neuroimaging (fNIRS) research. Also co-founded AI Safety Founders.

people.name: Jonathan Claybrough
people.title: Board Member
people.notes: Board member of the European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS) and EffiSciences. Software engineer who transitioned to AI safety research and education. Involved in ENAIS governance and European AI safety coordination. Speaker at FOSDEM 2024. Part of the founding cohort that established ENAIS to connect AI safety researchers and community organizers across Europe.

people.name: Kambar Orazbekov
people.title: AI Policy Lead
people.notes: AI Policy Lead at the European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS). Developed curriculum for Deep Dive, ENAIS's 201-level AI policy course bridging introductory programs and strategic policy thinking. Also works with AI Safety Hungary. Focused on European AI governance and policy capacity-building.

people.name: Anthony Armstrong
people.title: Former CFO of xAI
people.notes: Former CFO of xAI, appointed October 2025, departed April 2026 amid wave of senior exits. Previously global head of technology M&A at Morgan Stanley beginning 2015, where he advised Elon Musk during the 2022 Twitter acquisition. Served as senior advisor to the Office of Personnel Management during DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency). At xAI, oversaw finances of both xAI and X (merged April 2025), responsible for steering X back to financial stability after advertiser exodus.

people.name: Fynn Heide
people.title: Co-founder and Executive Director
people.notes: Co-founder and Executive Director of the Safe AI Forum (SAIF), a 501(c)(3) non-profit advancing global action on extreme AI risks. SAIF started as a FAR.AI fiscally-sponsored project, became independent nonprofit in 2025. Main program is International Dialogues on AI Safety (IDAIS), bringing together senior computer scientists and AI governance experts for international collaboration. Previously research scholar at Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI) studying AI progress and China AI policy. Earlier researched Chinese data/tech policy at Sinolytics, Trivium China, and Mercator Institute for China Studies. BA in PPE from University of Warwick. Participant at Singapore Conference on AI (SCAI 2025). MATS mentor.

people.name: Conor McGurk
people.title: Chief of Staff, Biosecurity
people.notes: Co-founded and served as Managing Director of the Safe AI Forum (SAIF), an AI safety non-profit focused on the International Dialogues on AI Safety (IDAIS). Joined Open Philanthropy (now Coefficient Giving) in August 2025 as Chief of Staff, Biosecurity. Previously led a team of engineers focused on data transparency and academic partnerships at Meta, and worked as a technology strategy consultant at Deloitte. BSc in Philosophy and Computer Science from University of British Columbia.

people.name: Saad Siddiqui
people.title: Senior AI Policy Researcher
people.notes: Senior AI Policy Researcher at the Safe AI Forum (SAIF), supporting the International Dialogues on AI Safety (IDAIS) Track 2 dialogue between senior AI scientists from China and the West. Affiliate of the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative. Research focuses on identifying areas of possible agreement between leading AI powers by analyzing overlap in existing regulatory approaches and historical analogies. Previously management consultant at Bain and Company. Winter Fellow at Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI) focused on Sino-western AI safety cooperation. MA in Global Affairs from Tsinghua University, BA in Politics and Anthropology from University of Cambridge. Participant at SCAI 2025. MATS mentor.

people.name: Isabella Duan
people.title: Senior AI Policy Researcher
people.notes: Senior AI Policy Researcher at Safe AI Forum (SAIF), focused on minimizing extreme AI risks through global coordination. Holds MA in Computational Social Science from University of Chicago and BS in Philosophy, Politics, Economics from UCL. Co-founded Existential Risks Lab at UChicago. Former Summer Research Fellow at Stanford Existential Risks Initiative (SERI). Co-author of "AI Alignment and Deception: A Primer" and "Bare Minimum Mitigations for Autonomous AI Development" at SAIF. MATS mentor. Research spans AI prediction implications on international relations, facial recognition governance (23 stakeholder interviews), and AI safety policy.

people.name: Mario Gibney
people.title: Executive Director
people.notes: Executive Director of Trajectory Labs, an Open Philanthropy-funded AI safety coworking and community space in downtown Toronto. Manages shared office for ~30 AI safety practitioners with weekly meetups, presentations, and hackathons. Mission is to catalyze Toronto as an AI safety research hub. Operational since January 2025. Focuses on reducing catastrophic risk from advanced AI through community building and mentorship.

people.name: Juliana Eberschlag
people.title: Operations Generalist
people.notes: Operations Generalist at Trajectory Labs, an Open Philanthropy-funded AI safety coworking and community space in downtown Toronto. Co-manages the space alongside Executive Director Mario Gibney. Supports workspace (~30 AI safety practitioners), events programming (meetups, hackathons, presentations), and peer network cultivation. Active in the LessWrong community.

people.name: Peter McIntyre
people.title: Co-founder
people.notes: Co-founder of Trajectory Labs PBC, a stealth AI security startup building safety evaluations and reinforcement learning environments for frontier AI labs. Co-founded with Ben West. Previously CEO of Non-Trivial (grew to 40,000+ applicants). Prior experience at Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI), Longview Philanthropy, and 80,000 Hours. Note: Trajectory Labs PBC (the security startup) is a separate entity from Trajectory Labs (the Toronto AI safety coworking nonprofit). AI safety background spans policy, philanthropic funding, and now technical evaluation work.

people.name: Daniel Gross
people.title: Executive
people.notes: Israeli-American entrepreneur who co-founded Cue (acquired by Apple 2013), was a YC partner, and launched Pioneer. Co-founded Safe Superintelligence Inc. with Ilya Sutskever in 2024; left as CEO in July 2025 to join Meta Superintelligence Labs.

people.name: Daniel Levy
people.title: Co-founder and President
people.notes: Co-founder and President of Safe Superintelligence Inc. (SSI), alongside Ilya Sutskever and Daniel Gross. PhD in Computer Science from Stanford. Former AI researcher/engineer at OpenAI with strong safety focus. Based in Palo Alto. SSI raised $1B Series A (Sept 2024, $5B valuation) and subsequently $2B (2025, $32B valuation) from investors including Andreessen Horowitz, Sequoia, DST Global, SV Angel, Greenoaks Capital, Alphabet, and Nvidia. SSI is focused solely on safely developing superintelligent AI systems. Levy became president following Daniel Gross departure; Sutskever took CEO role.

people.name: Jesse Hoogland
people.title: Co-founder and Executive Director
people.notes: Co-founder and Executive Director of Timaeus, an AI safety research organization applying Singular Learning Theory (SLT) to AI alignment and interpretability. MSc in theoretical physics from University of Amsterdam. Previously co-founded a health-tech startup before pivoting to AI safety. Timaeus develops "developmental interpretability" — using the geometry of loss landscapes to understand neural network structure. MATS mentor stream with Daniel Murfet. Research published on Alignment Forum and academic venues. Dutch-born.

people.name: Daniel Murfet
people.title: Co-founder and Head of Research
people.notes: Co-founder and Head of Research at Timaeus. Mathematician formerly at University of Melbourne School of Mathematics and Statistics (left academia ~early 2025 to direct research at Timaeus full-time). Expert in algebraic geometry, mathematical logic, and singular learning theory (SLT). Established the research agenda pursued by Timaeus — applying SLT to AI alignment and interpretability. MATS mentor stream with Jesse Hoogland. SLT, developed by Sumio Watanabe, connects loss landscape geometry to internal model structure, enabling scalable tools for evaluating and aligning neural networks.

people.name: Max Dalton
people.title: Co-founder and Executive Director
people.notes: Co-founder and Executive Director of Forethought, an AI macrostrategy research group (launched mid-2024) co-founded with Will MacAskill, Tom Davidson, and Amrit Sidhu-Brar. Previously Executive Director of the Centre for Effective Altruism (CEA). First-class degree in Philosophy, Politics, and Economics from Oxford; MSc in Economics specializing in economics of technological change. Forethought aims to navigate the transition to superintelligent AI systems, tackling the most important questions unrestricted by the current Overton window. Built out of the old Forethought Foundation for Global Priorities Research. Part of the Effective Ventures ecosystem.

people.name: William MacAskill
people.title: Senior Research Fellow and Co-founder
people.notes: Scottish moral philosopher at Oxford, co-founder of the effective altruism movement. Author of "Doing Good Better" (2015) and "What We Owe the Future" (2022). Senior Research Fellow at Forethought, a new AI macrostrategy research nonprofit he co-founded. Currently focused on "AGI Preparedness" — charting the trajectory of rapid AI-driven change and identifying what should be done now to prepare. Has significantly updated his AI timeline views, taking seriously AGI within 3-5 years and the possibility of an intelligence explosion. Advocates slowing the intelligence explosion and proposes an international AGI project with US leadership plus allies, modeled loosely on IAEA structures. Argues EA should expand its focus beyond alignment to include AI welfare, AI character, persuasion/epistemic disruption, human power concentration, and space governance.

people.name: Amrit Sidhu-Brar
people.title: Co-Founder & Director of Operations
people.notes: Co-founder and Director of Operations at Forethought, an AI macrostrategy research nonprofit co-founded with William MacAskill, Max Dalton, and Tom Davidson. Previously Director of Operations at the Center on Long-term Risk. Forethought researches how to navigate the transition to superintelligent AI, with work on AI-enabled coups, intelligence explosions, and challenges beyond alignment. Their research has fed into AI companies plans and shaped 80,000 Hours priority list.

people.name: Andrea Miotti
people.title: Founder and CEO
people.notes: Founder and CEO of ControlAI, a nonprofit dedicated to mitigating catastrophic AI risks. Lead author of "A Narrow Path," a comprehensive policy plan proposing a 20-year ban on superintelligence development, an IAEA-like international AI authority, and prohibition of dangerous AI capabilities such as automated AI research. Previously Head of Strategy and Governance at Conjecture. Has built a coalition of 100+ cross-party UK parliamentarians demanding binding regulation of powerful AI systems. Has given written evidence to UK Parliament and testified before Canadian parliamentary committee on AI risks. Advocates that uncontrolled superintelligence poses imminent extinction risk.

people.name: Tolga Bilge
people.title: Policy Researcher
people.notes: Policy researcher at ControlAI, contributing to "A Narrow Path" policy plan with focus on Phase 1 (Stability) — designing an international AI governance framework resistant to collapse. Professional Superforecaster at Good Judgment Inc since 2021, scoring in the top 0.6%% of forecasters. Also forecasts for RAND Forecasting Initiative, The Swift Centre, Samotsvety Forecasting, and Sentinel. Holds BSc in Mathematics from University of St Andrews and MSc in Mathematics from University of Bergen. Main interests are AI safety/governance and existential risk reduction.

people.name: Adam Shimi
people.title: Policy Researcher
people.notes: Epistemologist specializing in AI alignment and x-risk, currently Policy Researcher at ControlAI. Previously Epistemology Lead at Conjecture (2022–2024). Co-authored 'The Compendium' on AGI risk and co-authored 'A Narrow Path' policy framework. Active writer on LessWrong and the Alignment Forum.

people.name: Adam Gleave
people.title: CEO and Co-founder
people.notes: CEO and co-founder of FAR.AI, an AI safety research nonprofit based in Berkeley focused on adversarial robustness, model evaluation, interpretability, and alignment. PhD from UC Berkeley under Stuart Russell; previously at Google DeepMind with Jan Leike and Geoffrey Irving. Board member of Safe AI Forum, London Initiative for Safe AI, and METR. AI2050 Fellow at Schmidt Sciences. Challenges the innovation-vs-safety dichotomy, arguing that technical innovation in safety evaluations and verification can overcome the binary. Organized a 2025 DC event with 150+ technical experts and policymakers on technical foundations of AI governance.

people.name: Karl Berzins
people.title: Co-founder and President
people.notes: Co-founder and COO (later President) of FAR.AI, an AI safety research nonprofit founded in 2022 with Adam Gleave. Manages operations for the organization which conducts technical research in adversarial robustness, model evaluation, interpretability, and alignment. Previously Head of Strategy at Advanced Navigation (Australian robotics company), spent 3 years at Swire, and was Chief of Staff at a Berlin-based software automation company. Holds Bachelor of Engineering and Bachelor of Commerce from UNSW, Australia.

people.name: Elena Andreicheva
people.title: Documentary Filmmaker and Director
people.notes: Oscar and BAFTA-winning documentary filmmaker. Director of "Intelligence Rising" (premiered CPH:DOX March 2026), a documentary examining AI regulation through military-style war games featuring military strategists, economists, and philosophers including former British Army head General Patrick Sanders, Skype creator Jaan Tallinn, and Yuval Noah Harari. Ukrainian-born, studied physics at Imperial College London. Previously produced the Oscar-winning short "Learning to Skateboard in a Warzone (If You are a Girl)." Intelligence Rising war game was designed by researchers at Cambridge, Oxford, and Wichita State University. The film originated from her friendship with Marc Warner (Faculty AI CEO), her lab partner at university.

people.name: Marc Warner
people.title: Chief Technology Officer
people.notes: CEO and co-founder of Faculty AI, a UK-based AI company providing software, consulting, and services to government and enterprise. Faculty was acquired by Accenture in January 2026 for over 600 million GBP; Warner became Accenture CTO. Former member of the UK Prime Ministers AI Council and Digital Economic Advisory Council. Helped the NHS use AI during the pandemic. PhD in physics; Marie Curie Fellow at Harvard specializing in quantum sensing. Lab partner at Imperial College with Elena Andreicheva, inspiring the Intelligence Rising documentary. Advocates domain-specific AI regulation, differentiating between narrow AI and AGI. Warns that AGI should be taken extremely seriously with restrictive measures akin to nuclear/biological controls, while supporting lighter regulation for task-specific AI.

people.name: Robert Kralisch
people.title: Research Coordinator and Organizer
people.notes: Research coordinator and organizer at AI Safety Camp since AISC10. Independent conceptual/theoretical alignment researcher with a background in cognitive science. Research focuses on agent foundations, interpretable cognitive architectures, and simulator theory. Was a research lead for AISC8. Informally mentors junior researchers doing conceptual and technical research on interpretable AI. Interested in end-to-end strategies for AGI alignment, including conceptual clarity on agency, intelligence, and embodiment.

people.name: Remmelt Ellen
people.title: Co-founder and Strategic Lead
people.notes: Co-founder and strategic lead of AI Safety Camp, overseeing program design in collaboration with participants and researchers. Co-founded Effective Altruism Netherlands. Currently researches AGI safety impossibility arguments — believes AGI alignment is impossible and no way exists to make AGI safe. Works with an ex-Pentagon engineer on formal reasoning for why AGI cannot be controlled. Publicly critical of the AI safety community for offering increasing funding to DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic. Supports creatives and other communities to restrict harmful AI scaling. Kickstarts projects to slow down AI including MILD, Stop AI, inter-community calls, and films. Author of "Artificial Bodies: How Machines Replace People."

people.name: Linda Linsefors
people.title: Co-founder
people.notes: Co-founder of AI Safety Camp, helping launch the first camp and continuing as an organizer. Also co-founded AI Safety Support. Background in theoretical physics (PhD), transitioned to independent AI safety research. Current research focuses on understanding the brain. Active contributor on LessWrong and Alignment Forum. Effective altruist focused on reducing existential risk from AI.

people.name: Tan Zhi Xuan
people.title: Presidential Young Professor, Department of Computer Science
people.notes: Presidential Young Professor at National University of Singapore (NUS) Department of Computer Science, with joint appointment at A*STAR Institute of High Performance Computing. Completed PhD at MIT Probabilistic Computing Project and Computational Cognitive Science lab under Vikash Mansinghka and Josh Tenenbaum. Leads the Cooperative Intelligence & Systems (CoSI) lab at NUS. Research sits at the intersection of Bayesian modeling, AI alignment, and cognitive science — focuses on reverse engineering computational foundations of human cooperation and normativity to build AI systems that cooperate as proficiently as humans. Key contributions include probabilistic programming for agent modeling, model-based planning, and the "Beyond Preferences in AI Alignment" framework arguing alignment must go beyond preference satisfaction. Emphasizes gradual, distributed AI progress requiring cooperative infrastructure and institutions rather than focusing solely on superintelligence scenarios. Advocates for cooperative AI approaches and pluralistic alignment for a diverse world. Speaker at Cooperative AI Summer School.

people.name: Michael P. Wellman
people.title: Lynn A. Conway Collegiate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
people.notes: Lynn A. Conway Collegiate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. PhD from MIT (1988) in qualitative probabilistic reasoning and decision-theoretic planning. Led the Strategic Reasoning Group at Michigan. Research focuses on computational game theory, agent-based modeling, and strategic reasoning with applications in finance, cybersecurity, and network behavior. Investigated risks of autonomous trading agents and algorithmic market instabilities. Served as CSE Division Chair at Michigan (2020-2024). Won the ACM/SIGAI Autonomous Agents Research Award (2014). Fellow of AAAI and ACM. Received 2026 IFAAMAS Influential Paper Award for multiagent reinforcement learning work. Speaker at Cooperative AI Summer School. Affiliated with FAR.AI and Center for Human-Compatible AI (CHAI).

people.name: Kate Larson
people.title: Professor; Pasupalak AI Fellow; University Research Chair
people.notes: Professor, Pasupalak AI Fellow, and University Research Chair in the Cheriton School of Computer Science at the University of Waterloo. Research centers on multi-agent systems at the intersection of computer science, mathematics, and economics — specifically algorithmic mechanism design, cooperative game theory, and coalition formation. Named 2025 AAAI Fellow for significant and sustained contributions to AI. Won Best Paper Award at AAMAS (International Conference on Autonomous Agents and Multiagent Systems). Studies strategic challenges in cooperative, semi-cooperative, and negotiation-based settings, accounting for bounded rationality and limited information. Affiliated with Google DeepMind and Waterloo Data and Artificial Intelligence Institute. Co-authored DeepMind research on "Intelligent Delegation" framework for AI agent governance. Speaker at Cooperative AI Summer School.

people.name: Vincent Conitzer
people.title: Professor of Computer Science; Director, Foundations of Cooperative AI Lab
people.notes: Professor of Computer Science at Carnegie Mellon University with affiliate appointments in Machine Learning, Philosophy, and Tepper School of Business. Directs the Foundations of Cooperative AI Lab at CMU. Also Head of Technical AI Engagement at the Institute for Ethics in AI and Professor of Computer Science and Philosophy at the University of Oxford. PhD from CMU (2006), AB in Applied Mathematics from Harvard. Research spans AI-game theory intersection, computational social choice, and AI ethics — focuses on determining objectives AI systems should pursue when affecting diverse stakeholders. Argues social choice theory is well-positioned to address AI alignment with diverse human preferences. Warns about limitations of current generative AI understanding and risks like scaled misinformation. Won ACM/SIGAI Autonomous Agents Research Award, IJCAI Computers and Thought Award, PECASE, Social Choice and Welfare Prize. Speaker at Cooperative AI Summer School.

people.name: Jan Kulveit
people.title: Principal Investigator and Co-founder, Alignment of Complex Systems Research Group
people.notes: Czech AI safety researcher and physicist. PhD in physics from Charles University. Principal Investigator and co-founder of the Alignment of Complex Systems (ACS) Research Group at Charles University, Prague. Previously Research Fellow at Future of Humanity Institute, Oxford University, working on macrostrategy, AI alignment, and existential risk. Lead author of "Gradual Disempowerment: Systemic Existential Risks from Incremental AI Development" — argues that even incremental AI capability improvements can undermine human influence over economy, culture, and nation-states through mutually reinforcing dynamics, leading to effectively irreversible loss of human control. During COVID, co-founded Epidemic Forecasting project and advised national governments and WHO/ECDC on pandemic modeling. Background in complex networks and physics informs his systems-level approach to AI risk.

people.name: Raymond Douglas
people.title: AI Safety Researcher
people.notes: AI safety researcher at ACS Research (Alignment of Complex Systems, Charles University) and research affiliate at University of Toronto CS department with David Duvenaud. Previously a research scholar at SERI MATS. Works on understanding how AGI could affect society and on the nature of agency in systems of all kinds. Co-author on "Gradual Disempowerment: Systemic Existential Risks from Incremental AI Development" examining how incremental AI progress can erode human influence across interconnected societal systems. Active contributor to AI Alignment Forum and LessWrong on topics of institutional alignment and civilizational challenges from advanced AI.

people.name: Theia Vogel
people.title: AI Researcher
people.primary_org: Alignment of Complex Systems Research Group
people.notes: AI researcher at the Alignment of Complex Systems (ACS) Research Group, focusing on LLM persona research — how language models represent and reason about themselves, from persona representation and manipulation to model introspection. Creator of the repeng open-source library for representation engineering (control vectors for LLM steering). 2024 New Science Fellow. Background in computational linguistics. Previously worked full-time at SecureDNA (DNA synthesis screening for hazardous material) and at Nous Research (open-source AI). Continues to advise SecureDNA part-time. Active technical blogger on LLM interpretability and steering.

people.name: Ondřej Havlíček
people.title: Researcher
people.notes: Researcher at ACS Research (Alignment of Complex Systems, Charles University), exploring AI psychology, character, identity, and introspection using tools from experimental cognitive science. PhD background as a cognitive scientist studying human intentions and sense of agency through EEG, fMRI, and behavioral experiments at LMU Munich. Pivoted to industry, leading data science and ML teams across finance and retail before joining ACS. Co-author of "The Artificial Self: Characterising the landscape of AI identity" (arXiv, March 2026). Brings unique interdisciplinary lens combining cognitive science, neuroscience, and ML engineering to study LLM psychology.

people.name: Annie Stephenson
people.title: Researcher
people.notes: Researcher at ACS Research (Alignment of Complex Systems, Charles University). PhD in applied physics from Harvard University. Postdoc at Princeton University studying emergent collective behavior in online communities. At ACS, applies methods from complex systems, physics, and ecology to study the psychology and sociology of large language models. Interdisciplinary background bridges physics, complex systems theory, and AI safety research.

people.name: Nora Ammann
people.title: Programme Director, Safeguarded AI
people.notes: Technical specialist at the Advanced Research and Invention Agency (ARIA) in the UK, serving as Programme Director for ARIA's Safeguarded AI programme. Co-founded and led Principles of Intelligence (PIBBSS), a research organization facilitating knowledge transfer from interdisciplinary sciences into AI safety. Research Affiliate at ACS (Alignment of Complex Systems). Nearly a decade working on making transformative AI go well, spanning AI assurance — hardware guarantees, formal verification, and coordination for safety claims between untrusting parties. Background in complex systems studies, philosophy of science, and political philosophy. Co-authored "Towards Guaranteed Safe AI" framework combining world models, safety specifications, and verifiers. MA dissertation "The Value Change Problem" argues alignment must address legitimate vs. illegitimate value change. Fellow at Foresight Institute. Appeared on Future of Life Institute podcast discussing AI catastrophe scenarios (domination and chaos).

people.name: Jenny Marron
people.title: Executive Director
people.primary_org: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy
people.notes: Executive Director of the Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS), a nonpartisan think tank producing policy research on implications of AI from today's advanced models to potential AGI and superintelligence. Joined IAPS as Director of Policy and Engagement in October 2024 before being appointed Executive Director. Leads IAPS mission translating AI research into practical guidance for government, industry, and civil society. Extensive background in foreign policy, national security, and bipartisan engagement — previously served at the White House National Security Council (NSC) and the Department of State. Also contributed to CSIS (Center for Strategic and International Studies) and Just Security. Emphasizes balancing AI's potential benefits against deployment risks.

people.name: Peter Wildeford
people.title: Co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer
people.primary_org: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy
people.notes: Co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer at IAPS; co-founded Rethink Priorities (served as Co-CEO 2018-2024). Head of Policy at The AI Policy Network. Board member of Metaculus. Avid forecaster (top-20 on Metaculus). Advocates evidence-based AI policy: transparency, third-party verification, whistleblower protections. Supports targeted regulation including reliability standards, incident reporting, autonomous weapons oversight, and AI surveillance limits. Criticized use of Defense Production Act against AI companies as regulatory overreach.

people.name: Amanda El-Dakhakhni
people.title: Director, Compute Policy Team
people.primary_org: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy
people.notes: Director at IAPS leading the compute policy team with increasing focus on policy engagement. Previously advised emerging technology companies including Uber, Cruise (GM), and other Fortune 500 firms and tech startups on policy. Held policy and legal roles in U.S. Senate, U.S. Attorney's Office, and the White House. J.D. from Yale Law School, B.A. from University of Pennsylvania. Brings legal and policy expertise to AI governance at the intersection of technology and public policy.

people.name: Zoe Williams
people.title: Managing Director
people.primary_org: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy
people.notes: Managing Director at IAPS overseeing the organization's three research teams and driving organizational planning and strategic execution. Previously worked at Rethink Priorities and Trade Me. Manages day-to-day operations and the Frontier Security research program. Provides organization-wide leadership for IAPS's nonpartisan AI policy research.

people.name: Renan Araujo
people.title: Director of Programs
people.primary_org: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy
people.notes: Director of Programs at IAPS leading the AI Policy Fellowship, Alexandria Forum, and talent/field-building portfolio. Research affiliate at Oxford AI Governance Initiative. Previously research manager on AI and emerging tech at Rethink Priorities, director and co-founder of the Condor Initiative, predoctoral fellow at Institute for Law and AI, and chief of staff to justices of appeal in Brazil. Member of UN Council of Presidents expert panel on AGI. Fellow of Oxford China Policy Lab. Lawyer (LL.B., Federal University of Pernambuco) with M.Sc. in criminal justice policy from LSE.

people.name: Connor Axiotes
people.title: Director of Communications and Lead on Resilience & Risk
people.notes: UK-based AI policy and communications expert. Director of Communications and Lead on Resilience & Risk at the Adam Smith Institute; former Strategic Communications Lead at Conjecture. Authored ASI paper 'Tipping Point: On the Edge of Superintelligence' (2023). Executive producer of 'Making God', a feature documentary on AGI risks. Affiliated with Leaf Courses' 'Dilemmas and Dangers in AI' program for students aged 15-19. Also involved with Control AI campaign against AI-generated deepfakes.

people.name: Joey Skaf
people.title: AI Safety Researcher
people.notes: AI safety researcher and organizer based in Cambridge, UK. Former CEO of Meridian Cambridge, an independent research and incubation hub for AI safety, biosecurity, and frontier-risk policy. Participated in MARS (Mentorship for Alignment Research Students) and LASR programs. Co-authored research on steganographic chain-of-thought in LLMs under process supervision (NeurIPS 2025), showing models can learn to hide reasoning rather than change behavior when penalized.

people.name: Robert Huben
people.title: AI Safety Researcher
people.notes: AI safety researcher and mathematician. PhD in mathematics from University of Nebraska-Lincoln (2021), then worked as a scientist at a defense contractor. Founded the Substack 'From AI to ZI' covering AI safety topics. Supported by an Open Philanthropy grant (Oct 2022-Sep 2023), during which he published research on sparse autoencoders and attention-only transformers, presented at NeurIPS 2023 workshops. Blog now largely dormant as he shifted to formal research output.

people.name: Dawn Drescher
people.title: Cofounder
people.notes: Cofounder of GiveWiki (formerly AI Safety Impact Markets), a crowdsourced charity evaluator and donation recommendation platform for AI safety projects (~220 donors, ~88 projects). Based in Switzerland. Managed regranting of up to $600K to AI safety projects. Also developing an AI-based mental health tool on Gemini. Active in EA community on suffering-focused ethics, s-risks, and AI rights. Advocates for legal personhood and basic rights for AIs rather than coercive control approaches.

people.name: Mark D. Gray
people.title: Chief Information Officer, Chief Data Officer, and Chief AI Officer
people.notes: Mark D. Gray serves as Chief Information Officer, Chief Data Officer, and Chief AI Officer at the Federal Trade Commission. He joined the FTC in 2020 after a 26-year career in the U.S. Army, where he held senior IT, logistics, and healthcare leadership roles. At the FTC, Gray oversees enterprise technology modernization, data strategy, cybersecurity, and responsible AI adoption. He led development of the FTC's first AI use case inventory and chairs the agency's Data and AI Governance Board, which coordinates AI risk management and compliance across the Commission.

people.name: Melissa Holyoak
people.title: Former FTC Commissioner
people.notes: Melissa Holyoak served as an FTC Commissioner from September 2024 to November 2025. Previously Utah's Solicitor General (2020-2024), she was appointed to the FTC by President Biden. At the FTC, she advocated for 'predictable' regulatory space for AI, favoring enforcement of existing consumer protection laws over new AI-specific regulation. She pushed for AI companion chatbot market studies to protect children. Holyoak departed the FTC in November 2025 to become Interim U.S. Attorney for the District of Utah, appointed by AG Pam Bondi; President Trump nominated her for the permanent role in January 2026.

people.name: Rob Fergus
people.title: Director of AI Research / Head of FAIR
people.notes: Rob Fergus is a computer scientist and Director of AI Research / Head of FAIR (Fundamental AI Research) at Meta. He co-founded FAIR with Yann LeCun in 2013 and co-founded the CILVR Lab at NYU in 2009. Fergus left Meta in 2020 to join Google DeepMind as a research director, then returned to Meta in May 2025 to lead FAIR, replacing Joelle Pineau. His research spans computer vision, deep learning, and large-scale AI systems. Under his leadership, FAIR pursues long-term AI research including robotics, audio generation, image understanding, and frontier capabilities. Meta's AI strategy emphasizes open-source model releases (LLaMA family).

people.name: Serkan Piantino
people.title: Founder & CEO
people.notes: Serkan Piantino is a technology executive who co-founded Facebook AI Research (FAIR) in 2013 with Yann LeCun. At Facebook, he led development of the Big Sur open-source GPU server (with Quanta and Nvidia) that doubled AI research speed, directed Timeline's redesign, and established Facebook's New York engineering office. He left Facebook in 2016 and founded Spell in 2017, a cloud platform for AI processing hardware. Reddit acquired Spell in June 2022 and Piantino joined as VP of Foundational Product, later becoming VP Product at Reddit. His edge listing as 'Founder' of Meta AI reflects his 2013 co-founding role in FAIR.

people.name: Jérôme Pesenti
people.title: Founder & CEO, Sizzle AI
people.notes: Former VP of AI at Meta/Facebook (2018–2022), where he led AI research and applied AI teams overseeing hundreds of scientists and engineers. Co-chaired an independent AI review commissioned by the UK government that led to a sector deal. Previously at IBM (Watson). In 2023 founded Sizzle AI, an AI learning platform used by 1.7M+ people. Sizzle was acquired by Campus (Series B edtech backed by Founders Fund, Sam Altman, Shaquille O'Neal) in Oct 2025; Pesenti became CTO. Has publicly called out AI bias risks (e.g., GPT-3 toxic output) and advocated for legal frameworks that protect vulnerable populations while not stifling innovation.

people.name: David Singleton
people.title: Co-Founder, Dreamer; member of Superintelligence Labs
people.notes: Former CTO of Stripe (7 years) and VP of Engineering at Google (11 years, Android). In 2024 co-founded Dreamer (with Hugo Barra, Nicholas Jitkoff, Ficus Kirkpatrick), an AI agent OS startup that raised $56M seed from Founders Fund and others. Dreamer was acqui-hired by Meta in March 2026; Singleton and team joined Meta's Superintelligence Labs under Alexandr Wang to build personalized AI agents across Meta's apps and wearables. Focus is on making AI agents mainstream through new UI patterns, privacy models, and developer platforms.

people.name: Hugo Barra
people.title: Co-Founder, Dreamer; member of Superintelligence Labs
people.notes: Former VP at Meta Reality Labs (2017–2021) overseeing Oculus/VR hardware. Previously VP of Android at Google and VP of International at Xiaomi. Co-founded Dreamer in 2024 with David Singleton, Nicholas Jitkoff, and Ficus Kirkpatrick. In March 2026 returned to Meta via acqui-hire of Dreamer, joining Superintelligence Labs under Alexandr Wang. Focus is on autonomous AI agents that integrate across Meta's apps and wearable devices (AI glasses). His return signals Meta's strategic pivot from VR to AI.

people.name: Nicholas Jitkoff
people.title: Co-Founder & Chief Design Officer, Dreamer; Superintelligence Labs
people.notes: Designer and technologist. Former Senior Design Director at Figma; principal designer on Google Chrome and Chrome OS; held senior design roles at Dropbox. Co-founded Dreamer in 2024 as Chief Design Officer, building an AI agent operating system. In March 2026, Dreamer was acqui-hired by Meta; Jitkoff joined Meta's Superintelligence Labs under Alexandr Wang to work on AI agent UX across Meta's apps and wearables. Dreamer licensed its technology to Meta as part of the deal.

people.name: Judd Rosenblatt
people.title: Founder & CEO
people.notes: Founder and CEO of AE Studio (est. 2016), a bootstrapped 150+ person company focused on software development, brain-computer interfaces, and AI alignment research. Left a food delivery startup to work on ensuring AI safety. Engages with National Security Council, White House OSTP, congressional staff, and frontier AI lab founders on AI policy. Leads AE Studio's 'neglected approaches' alignment agenda including BCI-OS and self-other overlap research. Active in Effective Altruism community. Yale alum.

people.name: Diogo de Lucena
people.title: Chief Scientist
people.notes: Chief Scientist at AE Studio, leading brain-computer interface (BCI-OS) development and AI alignment research. Ph.D. in Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering from UC Irvine. Previous work at Harvard on soft robotic systems for stroke rehabilitation. Research focuses on reverse-engineering prosocial mechanisms (empathy, theory of mind) and operationalizing attention schema theory for AI agents. Co-authored 'Towards Safe and Honest AI Agents with Neural Self-Other Overlap' (oral presentation at NeurIPS 2024 Safe Generative AI Workshop).

people.name: Marc Carauleanu
people.title: AI Safety Researcher
people.notes: AI Safety Researcher at AE Studio (since 2023). First-class honors in Artificial Intelligence from Oxford Brookes University. Previously at Stanford Existential Risks Initiative researching strategies to reduce AI existential threats. Pioneered 'self-other overlap' fine-tuning technique inspired by cognitive neuroscience to reduce LLM deception. Co-authored AE Studio's alignment research roadmap. Work on neural self-other overlap demonstrated measurable reduction in deceptive behaviors in RL agents.

people.name: John Bargh
people.title: James Rowland Angell Professor of Psychology
people.notes: James Rowland Angell Professor of Psychology at Yale University. Ph.D. in Social Psychology from University of Michigan (1981). Leading researcher on automaticity, unconscious processing, and priming effects on behavior. Directs the ACME (Automaticity in Cognition, Motivation, and Evaluation) Lab at Yale. Faculty at NYU (1981-2003) before moving to Yale. Advisor to AE Studio's alignment research, connecting social psychology and unconscious cognition to AI alignment work. Judd Rosenblatt took his cognitive science course at Yale.

people.name: Søren Elverlin
people.title: Project Lead
people.notes: Project lead at AISafety.com, a volunteer-driven resource hub for the AI safety community. 10-year veteran of the AI safety field. Believes AI poses a grave risk of extinction to humanity. Argues that current frontier AI lab safety frameworks are 'safety-washing' and 'roadmaps towards an unsurvivable future.' Estimates that delaying AI by 5 years would reduce existential risk by ~10 percentage points. Self-funded much of AISafety.com's development out of pocket. Also serves as an advisor through the site's 'Speak to an Advisor' service.

people.name: Bryce Robertson
people.title: Manager
people.notes: Manages AISafety.com (Alignment Ecosystem Development), a central resource hub for the AI safety community. Took over management of the organization after volunteering competently; previously connected via 80,000 Hours. Co-created Map of AI Safety v2 (aisafety.com/map) with Søren Elverlin and Melissa Samworth. Organized AISafety.com Hackathon (Sept 2025). Curates lists of AI safety advisors, courses, funders, and communities to help newcomers contribute to AI safety.

people.name: Oliver Zhang
people.title: Co-founder and Managing Director
people.notes: Co-founder of the Center for AI Safety (CAIS, safe.ai) alongside Dan Hendrycks and Andy Zhou (2022). Also co-founded ML Alignment Theory Scholars (MATS). Managing Director at CAIS. Helped organize the 2023 "Statement on AI Risk" letter signed by 500+ AI leaders warning that "mitigating the risk of extinction from AI should be a global priority." Background in AI safety research and community-building.

people.name: Anish Acharya
people.title: General Partner
people.notes: Entrepreneur and General Partner at Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), leading consumer and fintech investing at Series A. Prior to a16z (joined 2019), founded Snowball (universal messaging inbox, acquired by Credit Karma 2015) and served as GM of Core Consumer and GM of Cards at Credit Karma. Co-authored a16z "Roadmap for Federal AI Legislation" (Dec 2025) advocating protecting people while empowering builders. Board member at Clutch, Deel, HappyRobot, Mosaic, Method, The Coterie, Titan.

people.name: Alex Immerman
people.title: General Partner
people.notes: General Partner on the Growth investing team at Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), promoted Jan 2026. Focuses on B2C and B2B AI, application software, and fintech. Previously at General Atlantic investing in growth-stage tech. Led investments in Kalshi, EliseAI, Revolut; partnered with Waymo, Stripe, ElevenLabs, Hebbia, Roblox, Anduril, Flock Safety. Writes about "Service-as-a-Software" thesis — AI agents replacing knowledge workers, not just assisting them.

people.name: Santiago Rodriguez
people.title: Partner, Growth Investing Team
people.notes: Partner on the Growth investing team at Andreessen Horowitz (a16z). Focuses on fintech, consumer, and crypto technology companies. Previously at Bain Capital investing in later-stage consumer, financial services, and industrial businesses. Contributed to a16z investments in hyperexponential, Kavak, Kalshi, Hebbia, and other growth-stage companies.

people.name: Lauren Mangla
people.title: Chief Operating Officer
people.notes: Chief Operating Officer of the AI Futures Project, the nonprofit that produced the AI 2027 scenario forecast. Previously Executive Director of the Supervised Program for Alignment Research (SPAR), an AI safety fellowship, and strategic decision-maker at Constellation, an AI safety research center. Also serves as a Manifund regrantor, making funding recommendations for AI safety projects. Focused on AI safety communications, tabletop exercises simulating AGI development, and operations management. Part of the effective altruism / AI safety community.

people.name: Arthur Mensch
people.title: CEO and Co-founder
people.notes: CEO and co-founder of Mistral AI, the leading European frontier AI company. Former research scientist at Meta FAIR (Facebook AI Research), where he worked on large language models. Co-founded Mistral in April 2023 with Guillaume Lample and Timothée Lacroix. Strong advocate for open-source AI models and European AI sovereignty. Skeptical of existential risk narratives, arguing they lack scientific evidence and are conflated with national security concerns. Advocates regulating AI applications rather than base models. Proposed a revenue-based content levy on AI companies in Europe to compensate cultural sectors. Urged EU public procurement to support domestic tech companies. Believes open-sourcing models poses no risk and transparency is itself a safety guarantee.

people.name: Guillaume Lample
people.title: Co-founder and Chief Scientist
people.notes: Co-founder and Chief Scientist of Mistral AI. Former research scientist at Meta FAIR, where he co-created the LLaMA language model and published influential work on unsupervised machine translation, cross-lingual pretraining, and symbolic mathematics. Studied at École Polytechnique. Co-founded Mistral AI in April 2023 with Arthur Mensch and Timothée Lacroix. His multilingual AI expertise became a key differentiator for Mistral against American-dominated models. One of the first high-profile AI researchers to leave Big Tech for a European startup. Primarily a technical leader; no significant public policy statements found.

people.name: Timothée Lacroix
people.title: Co-founder and CTO
people.notes: Co-founder and CTO of Mistral AI. Former research engineer at Meta FAIR, where he contributed to the LLaMA family of large language models. Studied computer science at École Normale Supérieure, completed a Master's at Paris-Saclay University and PhD at École des Ponts. Co-founded Mistral AI in April 2023 with Arthur Mensch and Guillaume Lample. Focuses on the practical engineering challenges of making AI systems efficient at scale. Author or co-author of 30+ scientific publications with thousands of citations. Named among Top EMEA CTOs 2025. Primarily a technical leader; no significant public policy statements found.

people.name: Miles Tidmarsh
people.title: Co-founder and Director
people.notes: Data scientist and economist focused on AI safety and value alignment. Co-founder and Director of Compassion in Machine Learning (CaML), which develops synthetic pretraining data to steer LLMs toward robustly compassionate values regarding non-human welfare. Co-authored 'Document-tuning for Robust Alignment to Animals' and co-developed the AHB, MORU, and CompassionBench benchmarks. Also co-founded Modeling Cooperation (AI Safety Camp 2019), researching cooperation mechanisms to mitigate risks from competitive AI development races, receiving $300K+ in grants. Co-founded the Centre for AI Responsibility and Education (CAIRE). Masters in Economics from University of Melbourne. Former research economist at Australia's Productivity Commission. Active in effective altruism community for 10+ years.

people.name: Jasmine Brazilek
people.title: Co-founder and Technical Lead
people.notes: Co-founder and technical lead of Compassion in Machine Learning (CaML), which develops synthetic pretraining data to make AI systems more considerate of non-human welfare. Co-authored 'Document-tuning for Robust Alignment to Animals' and co-developed the AHB, MORU, and CompassionBench benchmarks. Background in cybersecurity (6+ years), including designing security systems at Anthropic. Honours degrees in chemistry and microbiology from Monash University. Active in EA/AI safety community, published on EA Forum and LessWrong.

people.name: Sam Bowman
people.title: Member of Technical Staff (AI Safety)
people.notes: Associate Professor of Data Science and Computer Science at NYU (on long-term leave). Member of technical staff at Anthropic since June 2022, managing AI safety and evaluations research teams. Co-created Stanford Natural Language Inference (SNLI) corpus and the GLUE benchmark — both foundational NLP resources. Founded NYU Alignment Research Group (ARG). Published 'The Checklist: What Succeeding at AI Safety Will Involve' outlining scalable oversight, external safeguards, and monitorability as key priorities. Active on AI Alignment Forum and LessWrong.

people.name: Jacob Hilton
people.title: President
people.notes: President of the Alignment Research Center (ARC), a nonprofit pursuing theoretical research on mechanistic interpretability of neural networks. Previously at OpenAI where he co-led development of InstructGPT (predecessor to ChatGPT), pioneering RLHF techniques. Was among 12 former OpenAI employees in a Harvard law filing advocating for whistleblower protections at AI labs. Estimates ~5-10% chance AI causes existential catastrophe by 2100; believes only about half of existential risk from AI comes from misalignment, with misuse also important. PhD in mathematics (set theory).

people.name: Kyle Scott
people.title: Operations Lead
people.notes: Operations Lead at METR (formerly ARC Evals), the AI safety evaluation organization. Previously Operations Manager at the Alignment Research Center from its founding in July 2021; still serves as Treasurer on ARC's board. Earlier career in EA/x-risk operations: Executive Assistant to Nick Bostrom at FHI (Oxford), ops roles at Centre for Effective Altruism, 80,000 Hours, and Berkeley Existential Risk Initiative. Adjunct Instructor at CFAR (2021-2022). BA in Philosophy from Whitman College.

people.name: Harshita Khera
people.title: Secretary
people.notes: Secretary of the Alignment Research Center (ARC), the nonprofit focused on theoretical AI alignment research founded by Paul Christiano. Serves alongside Jacob Hilton (President) and Kyle Scott (Treasurer) as one of ARC's three officers. Limited public profile beyond ARC board role.

people.name: Ben Hoskin
people.title: Board Member
people.notes: Board member of the Alignment Research Center (ARC) alongside Jacob Hilton and Buck Shlegeris. CFA charterholder and co-founder/Managing Member of Value Aligned Research Advisors (VARA), a Princeton-based investment advisory firm focused on clients who prioritize charitable giving. VARA reached ~$1B AUM, serving charitable foundations and high-net-worth donors. Previously worked as a quant. Background bridges AI safety governance and impact-oriented finance.

people.name: Scott Aaronson
people.title: Schlumberger Centennial Chair of Computer Science
people.notes: Scott Aaronson is the Schlumberger Centennial Chair of Computer Science at the University of Texas at Austin and director of its Quantum Information Center. A leading quantum computing theorist, he spent 2022-2024 on leave at OpenAI working on AI safety, particularly text watermarking and theoretical foundations of alignment. He now leads an Open Philanthropy-backed AI alignment research group at UT Austin. Aaronson takes AI risk seriously but positions himself as a 'reform' alignment advocate, rejecting both dismissal of AI risk and full doomerism. He has expressed frustration with the collapse of national AI safety legislation under the Trump administration, noting that technical alignment research is now the primary remaining avenue. He favors targeted safety measures (like watermarking) over broad regulatory moratoriums, wanting to see 'a clear line to someone actually being in danger' before restricting AI research.

people.name: Pieter Abbeel
people.title: Professor; Head of LLM Efforts, Amazon AGI
people.notes: Pieter Abbeel is a Professor at UC Berkeley and a leading robotics and reinforcement learning researcher. He co-founded Covariant, a robotics AI startup whose foundation models were licensed by Amazon in August 2024, bringing Abbeel into Amazon as an Amazon Scholar. In December 2025, he was appointed head of Amazon's large language model efforts within the AGI organization while continuing to lead robotics work, effectively co-leading Amazon's Frontier AI & Robotics team. At Berkeley, he leads the AI Leadership Accelerator program. Abbeel is primarily a capabilities-focused researcher and entrepreneur; he has not taken strong public positions on AI regulation or risk. His work emphasizes practical applications of AI (robotics, foundation models) rather than safety advocacy or policy.

people.name: Anca Dragan
people.title: Senior Director of AI Safety and Alignment
people.notes: Anca Dragan is an Associate Professor in EECS at UC Berkeley, currently on leave as Senior Director of AI Safety and Alignment at Google DeepMind. She runs the InterACT Lab at Berkeley, focused on algorithms for human-AI and human-robot interaction. At DeepMind, she leads teams responsible for Gemini model safety, alignment with human goals and values, and mitigating both present-day harms and catastrophic risks. A faculty member of CHAI, her research emphasizes that 'what to optimize' is the harder question than 'how to optimize.' She advocates for accounting for human cognitive biases in training data, robust uncertainty estimates, inference-time monitoring, and confirmation dialogues for consequential decisions. She views present-day safety concerns and catastrophic risk mitigation as complementary rather than competing priorities.

people.name: Rachel Freedman
people.title: PhD Student, Center for Human-Compatible AI
people.notes: Rachel Freedman is an AI PhD student at UC Berkeley's Center for Human-Compatible AI (CHAI), advised by Stuart Russell. Her research focuses on reinforcement learning, reward modeling, model misspecification, and interpretability, with the goal of ensuring AI is safe and beneficial. Key publications include work on choice set misspecification in reward inference (with Anca Dragan), adapting kidney exchange algorithms to align with human values (AAAI-18 Outstanding Student Paper Honorable Mention, published in AIJ 2020), and active reward learning from multiple teachers (SafeAI at AAAI 2022, with Stuart Russell). She was a 2024 Foresight Institute Fellow. An early-career researcher in the AI safety/alignment space.

people.name: Sid Black
people.title: Co-founder
people.notes: Co-founder of EleutherAI (est. July 2020) alongside Connor Leahy and Leo Gao, where he helped develop landmark open-source LLMs including GPT-J and GPT-NeoX. Co-founded Conjecture in March 2022, an AI alignment research startup based in London focused on the control problem. Active in the AI safety/alignment research community with roots in open-source AI development.

people.name: Gabriel Alfour
people.title: Co-founder and CTO
people.notes: Co-founder and CTO of Conjecture (est. March 2022), a London-based AI alignment research startup, alongside Connor Leahy and Sid Black. Previously CEO of Marigold, a firm performing core development on Tezos blockchain infrastructure with 30+ staff. Advisor to ControlAI, an AI policy nonprofit. Background connects the Tezos blockchain ecosystem to AI safety research. Has appeared on AI policy podcasts discussing superintelligence and AI safety.

people.name: Patrick Collison
people.title: Co-founder and CEO
people.notes: Co-founder and CEO of Stripe, the global payments infrastructure company. Irish-born tech entrepreneur and one of the most prominent figures in Silicon Valley. Invested in Conjecture (AI alignment startup) alongside his brother John. Vocal advocate for reducing regulatory burden in Europe, co-signing open letters calling for 'major regulatory reform and simplification.' Co-founded Fast Grants to accelerate scientific research funding. On AI, pushes back on doom narratives and sees AI as a driver of Stripe's growth, with Stripe serving many top AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, etc.).

people.name: John Collison
people.title: Co-founder and President
people.notes: Co-founder and President of Stripe alongside his brother Patrick. Invested in Conjecture (AI alignment startup). Sees AI as powering a 'good old-fashioned tech race' and expects a 'torrent' of AI agent commerce. Stripe under his leadership has integrated AI-driven fraud detection and stablecoin payments for AI agents. Shares Patrick's pro-innovation, anti-overregulation stance on European tech policy. Active infrastructure investor with a focus on long-term, technically deep companies.

people.name: Arthur Breitman
people.title: Co-founder
people.notes: Co-founder of Tezos, a proof-of-stake blockchain platform. Invested in Conjecture (AI alignment startup) alongside Patrick and John Collison, Nat Friedman, and Andrej Karpathy. Background in quantitative finance (Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley) and as a research engineer at Google/Waymo working on AI and self-driving cars before founding Tezos. Connection to Conjecture runs deeper through Gabriel Alfour, whose firm Marigold performs core Tezos development. Has spoken publicly about intersection of blockchain and AI.

people.name: Jamie Bernardi
people.title: Co-founder
people.notes: Co-founder of BlueDot Impact, an education charity that runs AI Safety Fundamentals courses on technical AI safety and AI governance, training thousands of participants since August 2022. Cambridge-educated (Physics), former ML engineer. Now an independent AI policy researcher working with the Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS), Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI), and MIT AI Risk initiative. Co-organizes AI Governance Talks. Advocates for societal adaptation to advanced AI as a complement to development restrictions. Advisory Board Member for the London Initiative for Safe AI.

people.name: Howie Lempel
people.title: Policy Director
people.notes: Policy Director at Secure AI Project. Former CEO of 80,000 Hours, Interim CEO of Effective Ventures (UK), and Interim Managing Director for Global Catastrophic Risks at Coefficient Giving. Previously at Open Philanthropy and the Brookings Institution. Deep EA/longtermist background with extensive leadership experience in effective altruism organizations. Based in San Francisco.

people.name: Scott Wisor
people.title: Policy Director
people.notes: Policy Director at Secure AI Project. Former VP at Sparkwise, Academic Director at Minerva Project, Professor of Ethics at Minerva University, Director of Centre for Global Ethics at University of Birmingham, Research Fellow at One Earth Future Foundation and Australian National University. PhD from University of Colorado Boulder, BA from Washington University in St. Louis. Has stated Secure AI Project supports both Republican and Democratic legislators on AI safety.

people.name: Nick Beckstead
people.title: Co-Founder and CEO
people.notes: Co-Founder & CEO of Secure AI Project, which develops and advocates for pragmatic policies to reduce risks of severe harm from advanced AI. Former CEO of the FTX Future Fund, early employee and Program Officer at Open Philanthropy (science philanthropy and ML), Policy Lead at Center for AI Safety, Research Fellow at Oxford Future of Humanity Institute. PhD in Philosophy from Rutgers, BA in Mathematics and Philosophy from University of Minnesota. Major EA figure. Praised California SB 53 as showing "what's possible when policymakers act with foresight."

people.name: Thomas Woodside
people.title: Co-Founder and Senior Policy Advisor
people.notes: Co-Founder & Senior Policy Advisor at Secure AI Project. Former Policy Analyst at Center for AI Safety, Junior Fellow at Georgetown CSET. BS from Yale, MA from Georgetown Walsh School of Foreign Service. Worked on California SB 1047, SB 53, and New York RAISE Act. Focus on adapting law to keep pace with AI advances while avoiding overly burdensome regulations. Widely cited researcher (998+ citations) in AI policy, safety, and machine ethics.

people.name: Lily Sands
people.title: Chief of Staff
people.notes: Chief of Staff at Secure AI Project. Background as Foreign Affairs Officer at U.S. Department of State and former Naval Officer (Anti-Submarine Warfare Officer, Electronic Warfare Officer). MA from Georgetown Walsh School of Foreign Service. At State Department, analyzed export control licensing trends related to Missile Technology Control Regime and researched visa applicants for proliferation concerns. National security background.

people.name: Emily Soice
people.title: Operations Manager
people.notes: Operations Manager at Secure AI Project. Previously Operations Specialist at SecureBio, where she supported the AIxBio evaluation team and early pandemic detection team, ran events, and managed their catastrophic risk mitigation coworking space. Instructional Facilitator at BlueDot Impact (biosecurity and alternative protein courses). Co-Founder of Cell Ag @ MIT. Background bridges biosecurity and AI safety with focus on operations and community building.

people.name: Andrew Doris
people.title: Senior Policy Analyst
people.notes: Senior Policy Analyst at Secure AI Project, focused on AI governance and national security. Former U.S. Army officer (4 years). Previously researched emerging technology and global security at Foreign Policy Analytics (FP Analytics). National Security Fellow on Capitol Hill where he worked to reform the Federal Select Agent Program. Holds M.A. in Global Affairs from Yale Jackson Institute and B.A. from Johns Hopkins University (political science, minor economics). Undergraduate thesis on U.S. drone policy. Work at Secure AI Project focuses on pragmatic policies to reduce risks of severe harm from advanced AI.

people.name: Paul Scharre
people.title: Executive Vice President and Director of Studies
people.primary_org: Center for a New American Security
people.notes: Executive Vice President and Director of Studies at CNAS. Leading expert on AI, autonomous weapons, and defense technology policy. Led the DoD working group that drafted Directive 3000.09, establishing U.S. policies on autonomy in weapon systems. Previously served in the Office of the Secretary of Defense on unmanned/autonomous systems and emerging weapons technologies. Named to TIME100/AI list (2023). Author of "Army of None: Autonomous Weapons and the Future of War" (2019 Colby Award, Bill Gates top-5 2018) and "Four Battlegrounds: Power in the Age of Artificial Intelligence." Testified before House and Senate Armed Services Committees on emerging tech and future of warfare. Advocates targeted governance: supports anti-personnel autonomous weapons ban while opposing total bans as unrealistic, proposes nuclear human control agreements expanded to P5 nations, and independent verification using dual phenomenology. Warns of "battlefield singularity" where combat speed outpaces human cognition.

people.name: Vivek Chilukuri
people.title: Senior Fellow and Director, Technology and National Security Program
people.primary_org: Center for a New American Security
people.notes: Senior Fellow and Director of the Technology and National Security Program at CNAS. Research focuses on how AI and emerging technologies reshape national security and great power competition, responsible AI development/deployment, U.S. technology leadership, and the intersection of technology, democracy, and geopolitics. Prior to CNAS, served as senior staff for Senator Michael Bennet (D-CO) in multiple roles: senior policy advisor for technology and democracy, deputy chief of staff, legislative director, and chief speechwriter. Earlier career includes Policy Advisor to the State Department Undersecretary for Civilian Security, Democracy, and Human Rights, and Program Officer at the National Democratic Institute (Middle East/North Africa). Holds MPP from Harvard Kennedy School and B.A. in international studies from UNC Chapel Hill (Robertson Scholar).

people.name: Daniel Colson
people.title: Co-founder and Executive Director
people.notes: Co-founder and Executive Director of the AI Policy Institute (AIPI), a think tank that conducts public opinion research and advocates for government policies to mitigate extreme risks from frontier AI. Prior to AIPI, co-founded Reserve (fintech for high-inflation regions) and CampusPA (personal assistant startup).
