organizations.notes: Major U.S. philanthropy founded by Andrew Carnegie in 1911. One of the largest private foundations with ~$4.5B in assets and ~$174M in annual grantmaking (FY 2023-24, 353 grants). Funds AI governance and technology policy through multiple channels: grants to CNAS (already an edge), AI-nuclear risk research ($8.2M package in Jan 2026 including grants to Arms Control Association and Institute for Security and Technology for the Andrew Carnegie AI-Nuclear Policy Accelerator), AI capacity building in African higher education ($900K to African Technology Policy Studies Network), AI ethics for families ($350K to Common Sense Media for AI ratings system), and AI-driven intelligence tools ($1M to Council on Foreign Relations for Open Source Observatory). Focus areas intersecting AI include international security, emerging technology governance, and responsible innovation. Program areas: International Peace and Security, Democracy, Education, and Higher Education.

organizations.name: Effective Ventures
organizations.notes: Umbrella organization for the effective altruism movement, comprising Effective Ventures Foundation (UK) and Effective Ventures Foundation USA, Inc. Provides fiscal sponsorship and operational support to high-impact projects. Renamed from CEA Ops in 2022. Sponsored projects include Centre for Effective Altruism (CEA), 80,000 Hours, Giving What We Can, EA Funds, Forethought Foundation, Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI, already an edge as fiscal sponsor), Longview Philanthropy, Asterisk, Atlas Fellowship, and Non-trivial. Budget exceeds $50M. Significant funder of AI safety and existential risk research through EA Funds, which was among the first funders of technical AI safety work. The Long-Term Future Fund specifically targets cause prioritization, existential risk mitigation, and safe AI development. Connected to FTX collapse via former leadership ties. Remains a central node in the EA/longtermist ecosystem directing resources toward AI governance and safety.

organizations.name: Information Technology Industry Council
organizations.notes: Major Washington DC-based tech industry trade association representing the world's largest technology companies. Premier global advocate for technology policy, with dedicated AI policy team. Developed an AI Accountability Framework and Global AI Policy Recommendations covering innovation, public trust, security/privacy, regulation, and global engagement. Supports risk-based, sector-specific AI regulation rather than broad restrictions; has argued lawmakers should limit regulation to avoid impeding industry growth. Advocates for federal preemption of state AI laws. Led coalition of 60+ organizations with ARI urging Congress to authorize the U.S. AI Safety Institute within NIST. Active in EU AI Act transparency code of practice drafting. Members include major tech companies.

organizations.name: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
organizations.notes: UK government department responsible for AI policy, created in February 2023. Houses the UK AI Safety Institute (AISI). Published a pro-innovation AI White Paper (March 2023) establishing five cross-sectoral principles: Safety/Security/Robustness, Transparency/Explainability, Fairness, Accountability/Governance, Contestability/Redress. Relies on principles-based guidance for existing sectoral regulators rather than dedicated horizontal AI legislation. Launched the AI Opportunities Action Plan (January 2025) focused on economic growth through AI adoption. Opened consultation on AI Growth Lab (October 2025) for cross-economy regulatory sandboxes. Plans comprehensive AI bill in a future parliamentary session rather than rushing to regulate. Secretary confirmed UK will legislate on AI risks but has not rushed binding measures.

resources.name: Situational Awareness
resources.title: Situational Awareness
resources.notes: 165-page essay series published June 2024 by Leopold Aschenbrenner, a former researcher on OpenAI's Superalignment team who was fired in April 2024. Argues AGI is "strikingly plausible" by 2027 by extrapolating GPT-2 → GPT-4 capability gains in compute, algorithmic efficiency, and "unhobbling," that a subsequent intelligence explosion could compress a decade of AI research into one year, and that the free world faces an existential race with the CCP over superintelligence. Became the defining national-security AI narrative in DC policy circles, heavily cited by hawks arguing for export controls, AI-lab security mandates, and a "Manhattan Project" posture on AI. Aschenbrenner has since launched an AGI-focused investment firm.
resources.author: Leopold Aschenbrenner
resources.key_argument: AGI is imminent; the US must win the race against China or face catastrophic consequences

resources.name: The AI Index Report 2024
resources.title: The AI Index Report 2024
resources.notes: Seventh annual AI Index Report, published April 2024 by Stanford's Institute for Human-Centered AI (HAI). The 2024 edition documented industry dominance of frontier ML research (51 notable models from industry in 2023 vs. 15 from academia), surging training costs (GPT-4 ≈ $78M, Gemini Ultra ≈ $191M), an ~8× jump in generative-AI private investment to $25.2B, a proliferation of national AI regulations, and introduced a new chapter on AI in science and medicine. Widely treated as the canonical reference on the state of the AI ecosystem; cited routinely in congressional testimony, executive-branch AI policy documents, and industry research.
resources.author: Stanford HAI
resources.key_argument: Comprehensive annual benchmarking of AI progress, investment, policy, and public opinion

resources.name: Why AI Will Save the World
resources.title: Why AI Will Save the World
resources.notes: Essay by Marc Andreessen (June 2023) arguing that AI will be a profoundly positive force for humanity, countering doomer narratives with a case for accelerating AI development.
resources.author: Marc Andreessen
resources.key_argument: AI risk is overstated by incumbents seeking regulatory moats; AI will generate enormous prosperity

resources.name: Governing AI: A Blueprint for the Future
resources.title: Governing AI: A Blueprint for the Future
resources.notes: White paper published May 25, 2023 by Microsoft under a foreword from Vice Chair and President Brad Smith. Part 1 lays out a five-point blueprint for AI public policy: build on government AI safety frameworks (e.g. NIST AI RMF), require "safety brakes" on AI systems controlling critical infrastructure, adopt a layered regulatory model distinguishing the applications / model / infrastructure layers of the tech stack, pursue transparency and a broader public AI governance architecture, and leverage AI to address societal challenges. Part 2 describes Microsoft's internal responsible-AI program. One of the first major corporate AI policy statements from a frontier-model developer; cited in congressional AI Insight Forums and in international AI governance coordination discussions.
resources.author: Microsoft (Brad Smith)
resources.key_argument: Proposes tiered governance framework based on AI capability levels

resources.name: Statement on AI Risk
resources.title: Statement on AI Risk
resources.notes: One-sentence open letter published May 30, 2023 by the Center for AI Safety (CAIS): “Mitigating the risk of extinction from AI should be a global priority alongside other societal-scale risks such as pandemics and nuclear war.” Signed by 350+ AI researchers and public figures, including Sam Altman, Demis Hassabis, Dario Amodei, Geoffrey Hinton, Yoshua Bengio, Stuart Russell, and Bill Gates. A watershed moment that moved existential AI risk from a fringe concern into mainstream policy discourse.
resources.author: Center for AI Safety (CAIS)
resources.key_argument: Mitigating the risk of extinction from AI should be a global priority alongside pandemics and nuclear war

resources.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
resources.title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
resources.notes: Voluntary framework published January 2023 by NIST (under Dept. of Commerce) providing guidance for organizations to manage risks from AI systems across the lifecycle. Built around four core functions — Govern, Map, Measure, Manage — and accompanied by the AI RMF Playbook and Generative AI Profile (July 2024). Widely adopted as the de facto U.S. industry reference; explicitly invoked in Biden's EO 14110 and in subsequent federal agency AI policies. Under the Trump administration's July 2025 AI Action Plan, NIST was directed to revise the RMF (removing references to DEI, misinformation, and climate change).
resources.author: NIST
resources.key_argument: Voluntary framework for managing AI risks throughout the AI lifecycle

resources.name: Stochastic Parrots Paper
resources.title: Stochastic Parrots Paper
resources.notes: "On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big? 🦜" — peer-reviewed paper presented at ACM FAccT 2021 by Emily M. Bender (U. Washington), Timnit Gebru, Angelina McMillan-Major, and Margaret Mitchell. Argues that ever-larger language models carry four underappreciated costs: environmental impact from compute, unaccountable bias baked into uncurated web-scale training data, research opportunity cost, and downstream harms from fluent but meaning-blind outputs (the "stochastic parrot" metaphor — pattern matching without understanding). Foundational text of the algorithmic-harms / AI ethics wing of the field; internal dispute over the paper precipitated Gebru's forced exit from Google in December 2020 and Mitchell's firing in February 2021, becoming a watershed moment in the politics of industry AI research.
resources.author: Emily Bender, Timnit Gebru, et al.
resources.key_argument: Large language models pose environmental, bias, and misinformation risks that are inadequately addressed

resources.name: The Alignment Problem
resources.title: The Alignment Problem
resources.notes: 2020 book by journalist and researcher Brian Christian (W.W. Norton, October 2020) structured in three parts: Prophecy (fairness, bias, and transparency failures in deployed ML systems — COMPAS, facial recognition, word embeddings), Agency (reinforcement learning, reward specification, and the difficulty of encoding objectives), and Normativity (imitation learning, inverse reward design, and long-horizon alignment of advanced AI). Widely cited as the most accessible bridge between mainstream ML-ethics literature and the AI-safety / long-term alignment community; frequently assigned as an introductory text in AI governance and technology-policy courses.
resources.author: Brian Christian
resources.key_argument: Comprehensive overview of the AI alignment challenge, from fairness to existential risk

resources.name: AI Frontiers
resources.title: AI Frontiers
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Platform from the Center for AI Safety (CAIS) posting articles written by experts from a wide range of fields discussing the impacts of AI.

resources.name: AI Policy Bulletin
resources.title: AI Policy Bulletin
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Publishes policy-relevant perspectives on frontier AI governance, including research summaries, opinions, interviews, and explainers.

resources.name: AI Prospects
resources.title: AI Prospects
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog by Eric Drexler on AI prospects and their surprising implications for technology, economics, environmental concerns, and military affairs.

resources.name: Astral Codex Ten
resources.title: Astral Codex Ten
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog covering many topics, including reasoning, science, psychiatry, medicine, ethics, genetics, economics, politics, and AI. Often book summaries and commentary on AI safety.

resources.name: Bounded Regret
resources.title: Bounded Regret
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog on AI safety by Jacob Steinhardt, a UC Berkeley statistics professor, analysing risks, forecasting future breakthroughs, and discussing alignment strategies.

resources.name: ChinaTalk
resources.title: ChinaTalk
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Deep, thoughtful coverage on China, technology, and US-China relations – written by Jordan Schneider.

resources.name: Cold Takes
resources.title: Cold Takes
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog about transformative AI, futurism, research, ethics, philanthropy etc. by Holden Karnofsky. Includes the "Most Important Century" post series. Last posted in 2024.

resources.name: Daniel Paleka's Newsletter
resources.title: Daniel Paleka's Newsletter
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog by AI safety researcher Daniel Paleka curating and succinctly analyzing the latest research and news in AI safety. Posts about every two months.

resources.name: DeepMind Safety Research
resources.title: DeepMind Safety Research
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog from the Google DeepMind safety team discussing research ideas about building AI safely.

resources.name: Don't Worry about the Vase
resources.title: Don't Worry about the Vase
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog by Zvi Mowshowitz on various topics, including AI, offering detailed analysis, personal insights, and a rationalist perspective. Posts very frequently.

resources.name: Miles's Substack
resources.title: Miles's Substack
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog from ex-OpenAI (now independent) AI policy researcher Miles Brundage on the rapid evolution of AI and the urgent need for thoughtful governance.

resources.name: Obsolete
resources.title: Obsolete
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Reporting and analysis on capitalism, great power competition, and the race to build superintelligence by freelance journalist Garrison Lovely.

resources.name: Paul Christiano's Blog
resources.title: Paul Christiano's Blog
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog on aligning prosaic AI by one of the leading AI safety researchers. No longer active but the archive is high quality.

resources.name: Planned Obsolescence
resources.title: Planned Obsolescence
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Substack by Ajeya Cotra, researcher at METR, covering AI capabilities forecasting, AI agent benchmarks, timelines for automating AI R&D, and implications for AI safety.

resources.name: Rising Tide
resources.title: Rising Tide
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog by Helen Toner (director of CSET and former OpenAI board member) offering analysis on navigating the transition to advanced AI systems.

resources.name: The Building Capacity Blog
resources.title: The Building Capacity Blog
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog about building the fields of AI safety and Effective Altruism, discussing big-picture strategy and sharing personal experience from working in the space.

resources.name: The Power Law
resources.title: The Power Law
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Top forecaster Peter Wildeford forecasts the future and discusses AI, national security, innovation, emerging technology, and the powers – real and metaphorical – that shape the world.

resources.name: Threading the Needle
resources.title: Threading the Needle
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Publication by Anton Leicht on the political economy of AI progress, featuring weekly posts on how institutions and political incentives interface with fast technological change.

resources.name: Victoria Krakovna's Blog
resources.title: Victoria Krakovna's Blog
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog by an AI safety researcher at Google DeepMind, covering alignment research, rationality, and personal productivity insights. Last AI post was 2023.

resources.name: AISafety.com: Media channels
resources.title: AISafety.com: Media channels
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. The AI safety space is changing rapidly. This directory of key information sources can help you keep up to date with the latest developments.

resources.name: AI Safety at the Frontier
resources.title: AI Safety at the Frontier
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Johannes Gasteiger, an alignment researcher at Anthropic, selects and summarizes the most interesting AI safety papers each month.

resources.name: AI Safety Events & Training
resources.title: AI Safety Events & Training
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Weekly newsletter listing newly-announced AI safety events and training programs, both online and in-person.

resources.name: AI Safety Funding
resources.title: AI Safety Funding
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Newsletter listing newly announced funding opportunities for individuals and organizations working to reduce AI existential risk.

resources.name: AI Safety in China
resources.title: AI Safety in China
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Newsletter from Concordia AI, a Beijing-based social enterprise, providing updates on AI safety developments in China.

resources.name: AI Safety Newsletter
resources.title: AI Safety Newsletter
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Newsletter published every few weeks discussing recent developments in AI and AI safety. No technical background required.

resources.name: Can We Secure AI With Formal Methods?
resources.title: Can We Secure AI With Formal Methods?
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Current-events newsletter for keeping up to date with FMxAI (formal methods and AI) with a gear toward safety, doing a mix of shallow technical reviews of papers and movement updates.

resources.name: Import AI
resources.title: Import AI
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Weekly updates on the latest developments in AI research (including governance) written by Jack Clark, co-founder of Anthropic.

resources.name: ML Safety Newsletter
resources.title: ML Safety Newsletter
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Infrequent newsletter aiming to inform readers about recent ML safety research, focussing on areas like adversarial robustness, interpretability, and control.

resources.name: Transformer
resources.title: Transformer
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Aiming to help decision-makers understand what’s happening in AI and why it matters – through news roundups, explainers, features, and opinion pieces.

resources.name: 80,000 Hours Podcast
resources.title: 80,000 Hours Podcast
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. In-depth conversations about the world’s most pressing problems – including AI safety – and what you can do to help solve them.

resources.name: AI X-risk Research Podcast
resources.title: AI X-risk Research Podcast
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Interviews with (mostly technical) AI safety researchers about their research, aiming to get a sense of why it was written and how it might reduce existential risk from AI.

resources.name: Dwarkesh Podcast
resources.title: Dwarkesh Podcast
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Well-researched interviews with influential intellectuals going in-depth on AI and technology, and their broader societal implications.

resources.name: Future of Life Institute Podcast
resources.title: Future of Life Institute Podcast
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Interviews with existential risk researchers, policy experts, philosophers, and a range of other influential thinkers.

resources.name: The AI Policy Podcast
resources.title: The AI Policy Podcast
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Podcast from the Center for Strategic & International Studies (CSIS) discussing AI regulation, innovation, national security, and geopolitics.

resources.name: The Cognitive Revolution
resources.title: The Cognitive Revolution
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Biweekly podcast where host Nathan Labenz interviews AI innovators and thinkers, discussing the transformative impact AI will likely have in the near future.

resources.name: AI Alignment Slack
resources.title: AI Alignment Slack
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. The biggest real-time online community of people interested in AI safety, with channels ranging from general topics to specific fields to local groups.

resources.name: AI Digest
resources.title: AI Digest
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Concise visual explainers of important trends in AI, grounded in concrete examples of what AI models can do right now.

resources.name: AI Risk Explorer
resources.title: AI Risk Explorer
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Online platform monitoring the emergence of large-scale AI risks, featuring curated information on evaluations, incidents, and policies.

resources.name: AI Risk: Why Care?
resources.title: AI Risk: Why Care?
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. AI safety chatbot that excels at addressing hard questions and counterarguments about existential risk.

resources.name: AI Safety for Fleshy Humans
resources.title: AI Safety for Fleshy Humans
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Accessible, comic-illustrated series describing the history of AI capabilities, the alignment problem, and potential solutions.

resources.name: AI Safety Map Anki Deck
resources.title: AI Safety Map Anki Deck
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Flashcards for helping to learn and memorize the main organizations, projects, and programs currently operating in the AI safety space.

resources.name: SB 53 (CA)
resources.title: SB 53 (CA)
resources.notes: California Senate Bill 53, an AI safety bill requiring developers of frontier AI models to implement shutdown capabilities and safety protocols for powerful AI systems.

resources.name: AI Safety Support
resources.title: AI Safety Support
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Field-building organization now chiefly serving as the home for an extensive resources list called Lots of Links.

resources.name: AI Watch
resources.title: AI Watch
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Database tracking people, organizations, and “products” in the AI safety community, serving as a reference for positions, affiliations, and related data.

resources.name: AISafety.com
resources.title: AISafety.com
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Hub for key resources for the AI safety community, including directories of courses, jobs, upcoming events and training programs etc. – and this map!

resources.name: Effective Altruism Domains
resources.title: Effective Altruism Domains
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Directory of domains freely available to be used for high-impact projects, including those contributing to AI safety.

resources.name: Effective Altruism Forum
resources.title: Effective Altruism Forum
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Forum on doing good as effectively as possible, including AI safety. Also has a podcast featuring text-to-speech narrations of top posts.

resources.name: LessWrong
resources.title: LessWrong
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Online forum dedicated to improving human reasoning, containing a lot of AI safety content. Also has a podcast featuring text-to-speech narrations of top posts.

resources.name: MIT AI Risk Repository
resources.title: MIT AI Risk Repository
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Comprehensive living database of over 1600 AI risks, categorized by their cause and risk domain.

resources.name: RiesgosIA.org
resources.title: RiesgosIA.org
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Spanish-language website featuring AI safety educational tools, including a daily‑updated monitor of AI safety research papers.

resources.name: AI Explained
resources.title: AI Explained
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. YouTuber discussing the latest AI developments as they happen, offering explanations and analysis of important research and events.

resources.name: AI In Context
resources.title: AI In Context
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Channel produced by 80,000 Hours presenting thoroughly researched, cinematic stories about what's happening in AI and where the trends are taking us.

resources.name: AI Safety Videos
resources.title: AI Safety Videos
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Comprehensive directory of AI safety video content, from beginner-friendly introductions to in-depth expert talks.

resources.name: Doom Debates
resources.title: Doom Debates
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Channel hosted by Liron Shapira featuring in-depth debates, explainers, and live Q&A sessions, focused on AI existential risk and other implications of superintelligence.

resources.name: Dr Waku
resources.title: Dr Waku
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Computer science PhD and AI research scientist talking about how AI will likely affect the world, along with expert interviews.

resources.name: RAISE Act
resources.title: RAISE Act
resources.notes: The New York Responsible AI Safety and Education (RAISE) Act, signed by Governor Hochul in December 2025, requiring frontier AI developers to publish and comply with safety and security governance plans.

resources.name: FAR․AI YouTube channel
resources.title: FAR․AI YouTube channel
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Talks given by AI safety experts at various events, covering topics ranging from mechanistic interpretability to evals and governance.

resources.name: Lethal Intelligence
resources.title: Lethal Intelligence
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. YouTube channel raising awareness about the lethal dangers of AGI through explainer videos and podcasts.

resources.name: Rational Animations
resources.title: Rational Animations
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Animated videos aiming to foster good thinking, promote altruistic causes, and help ensure humanity's future goes well – particularly regarding AI safety.

resources.name: Robert Miles
resources.title: Robert Miles
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. The most popular AI safety education channel, explaining technical alignment concepts to general audiences through accessible explainer videos.

resources.name: Siliconversations
resources.title: Siliconversations
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. YouTube channel explaining (mostly) AI safety concepts through entertaining stickman videos.

resources.name: Species
resources.title: Species
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Channel run by Drew Spartz educating a general audience about AI risk through high-effort mini-documentaries.

resources.name: The AI Risk Network
resources.title: The AI Risk Network
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. YouTube channel run by John Sherman, a Peabody and Emmy Award-winning former investigative journalist, aiming to make AI risk a kitchen table conversation.

resources.name: A Guide to the AI Tribes
resources.title: A Guide to the AI Tribes
resources.notes: Essay by Michel Justen (January 2026) mapping the major ideological factions in AI policy debate — their origins, assumptions, alliances, and conflicts over how AI should be developed and governed.
resources.author: Michel Justen

resources.name: The Left is Missing Out on AI
resources.title: The Left is Missing Out on AI
resources.notes: Essay by Dan Kagan-Kans (February 2026) arguing that the political left has ceded the AI conversation to the right by dismissing AI as overhyped, risking inability to shape one of the most consequential technological shifts in history.
resources.author: Dan Kagan-Kans

resources.name: AI 2027 scenario
resources.title: AI 2027 scenario
resources.notes: Scenario and forecast paper by Daniel Kokotajlo et al. projecting a plausible timeline for transformative AI development through 2027, exploring rapid capability gains and their implications for safety and governance.
resources.author: AI 2027 team

resources.name: AI as Normal Technology
resources.title: AI as Normal Technology
resources.notes: Essay by Arvind Narayanan and Sayash Kapoor arguing that AI should be understood as a transformative but ''normal'' technology — like electricity or the internet — rather than as an unprecedented existential force requiring extraordinary governance.
resources.author: Narayanan & Kapoor

resources.name: Dignity in a Digital Age
resources.title: Dignity in a Digital Age
resources.notes: Book by Rep. Ro Khanna (2022) advocating for policies that bring technology jobs and digital infrastructure to overlooked communities across America, bridging the gap between Silicon Valley and the rest of the country.
resources.author: Ro Khanna

resources.name: The Entrepreneurial State
resources.title: The Entrepreneurial State
resources.notes: Book by Mariana Mazzucato (2013) challenging the myth that innovation comes only from the private sector, arguing that government investment has been the driving force behind transformative technologies including the internet and smartphones.
resources.author: Mariana Mazzucato

resources.name: Power and Progress
resources.title: Power and Progress
resources.notes: Book by Daron Acemoglu and Simon Johnson (2023) examining how technological progress has historically benefited elites unless countered by deliberate institutional choices, and arguing for redirecting AI toward broadly shared prosperity.
resources.author: Acemoglu & Johnson

resources.name: Recoding America
resources.title: Recoding America
resources.notes: Book by Jennifer Pahlka (2023) diagnosing why government technology systems fail and outlining how modernizing digital infrastructure is essential for effective public services and democratic accountability.
resources.author: Jennifer Pahlka

resources.name: Governing the Commons
resources.title: Governing the Commons
resources.notes: Seminal book by Elinor Ostrom (1990) demonstrating that communities can successfully manage shared resources through self-governance without requiring privatization or top-down state control.
resources.author: Elinor Ostrom

resources.name: Techno-Optimist Manifesto
resources.title: Techno-Optimist Manifesto
resources.notes: Marc Andreessen''s manifesto (October 2023) published on the a16z website, making an expansive case for technological optimism and arguing against precautionary regulation of emerging technologies including AI.
resources.author: Marc Andreessen

resources.name: The Internet Con
resources.title: The Internet Con
resources.notes: Book by Cory Doctorow (2023) arguing that Big Tech companies maintain dominance through lock-in and switching costs, and proposing interoperability mandates as the key to breaking their grip on users and markets.
resources.author: Cory Doctorow

resources.name: Chip War
resources.title: Chip War
resources.notes: Book by Chris Miller (2022) tracing the geopolitical history of the global semiconductor industry and explaining how control over chip manufacturing has become a critical factor in international power dynamics and AI competition.
resources.author: Chris Miller

resources.name: Secure AI Project bills tracker
resources.title: Secure AI Project bills tracker
resources.notes: Online tracking tool maintained by the Secure AI Project that monitors and catalogs AI-related legislation at the state and federal level, providing real-time status updates on bills and regulatory proposals.
resources.author: Secure AI Project

resources.name: Dean Ball "How I Approach AI Policy"
resources.title: Dean Ball "How I Approach AI Policy"
resources.notes: Blog post by Dean Ball outlining his analytical framework for evaluating AI policy proposals, emphasizing pragmatic governance approaches that balance innovation with risk mitigation.
resources.author: Dean Ball

resources.name: Digitalist Papers Vol. 2
resources.title: Digitalist Papers Vol. 2
resources.notes: Volume 2 of The Digitalist Papers, published by the Stanford Digital Economy Lab (December 2025), a collection of 21 essays examining the economic implications of transformative AI, including proposals for universal basic capital and adaptive safety nets.
resources.author: Sandy Pentland et al.

resources.name: Alex Bores Congressional AI Framework
resources.title: Alex Bores Congressional AI Framework
resources.notes: Congressional AI policy framework released by New York Assemblyman Alex Bores (February 2026) as part of his campaign for Congress, proposing an eight-point national plan covering kids'' safety, data privacy, deepfakes, workforce impacts, and frontier AI safety testing.

resources.name: Trump AI Action Plan (July 2025)
resources.title: Winning the Race: America's AI Action Plan
resources.notes: On July 23, 2025, the White House released 'America's AI Action Plan,' a 25-page document focused on bolstering U.S. AI dominance through deregulation, ideologically neutral AI systems, infrastructure investment, and international competition. Released alongside three executive orders covering AI development, federal procurement, and infrastructure. The Action Plan was issued pursuant to Executive Order 14179 ('Removing Barriers to American Leadership in Artificial Intelligence,' signed January 23, 2025), which mandated the creation of a comprehensive AI action plan within 180 days. EO 14179 also revoked Biden's Executive Order 14110 on AI safety.

resources.name: Sacks federal preemption EO (Dec 2025)
resources.title: Sacks federal preemption EO (Dec 2025)
resources.notes: Executive order orchestrated by AI czar David Sacks (December 2025) asserting federal preemption over state-level AI regulations, aiming to create a unified national approach and prevent a patchwork of state AI laws.

resources.name: EU AI Act
resources.title: EU AI Act
resources.notes: The European Union''s comprehensive AI regulation framework, entered into force in August 2024, establishing a risk-based classification system for AI applications with tiered requirements ranging from transparency obligations to outright bans on certain uses.

resources.name: New Consensus AI platform
resources.title: New Consensus AI platform
resources.notes: New Consensus is a progressive economic think tank whose AI platform advocates for public investment and democratic governance of artificial intelligence.

resources.name: Secure AI Project state bill tracker
resources.title: Secure AI Project state bill tracker
resources.notes: A tracking tool maintained by the Secure AI Project that monitors and catalogs state-level AI legislation across the United States.

resources.name: Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights
resources.title: Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights
resources.notes: Published by the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy in October 2022, this non-binding framework outlines five principles to protect the public from harms caused by automated systems.

resources.name: SB 1047 (CA, vetoed)
resources.title: SB 1047 (CA, vetoed)
resources.notes: California Senate Bill 1047, authored by Senator Scott Wiener, would have imposed safety requirements on developers of frontier AI models; it was vetoed by Governor Newsom in September 2024.

resources.name: Colorado AI anti-discrimination law
resources.title: Colorado AI anti-discrimination law
resources.notes: Colorado SB 24-205, signed into law in 2024, is the first U.S. state law specifically regulating algorithmic discrimination in high-risk AI decision-making systems.

resources.name: Human Compatible
resources.title: Human Compatible
resources.notes: 2019 book by UC Berkeley computer scientist Stuart Russell arguing that the standard model of AI — machines pursuing fixed objectives — is fundamentally unsafe as capability scales, and proposing a provably-beneficial alternative in which AI systems remain uncertain about human preferences and defer to human feedback. Widely cited in AI governance discussions of alignment, oversight, and the case for safety-focused regulation. A foundational popular-audience text alongside Bostrom's Superintelligence.
resources.author: Stuart Russell
resources.key_argument: Current AI paradigm of fixed objectives is fundamentally dangerous; need provably beneficial AI

resources.name: The Precipice: Existential Risk and the Future of Humanity
resources.title: The Precipice: Existential Risk and the Future of Humanity
resources.notes: A 2020 book by philosopher Toby Ord arguing that safeguarding humanity''s long-term future from existential risks, including advanced AI, is among the most important moral priorities of our time.
resources.author: Toby Ord
resources.key_argument: Argues this century is a critical period for existential risk, with unaligned AI as the greatest threat.

resources.name: The Urgency of Interpretability
resources.title: The Urgency of Interpretability
resources.notes: A paper or talk making the case that understanding the internal workings of AI models (interpretability) is an urgent priority for ensuring safe and trustworthy AI systems.
resources.author: Dario Amodei
resources.key_argument: Understanding how AI models work internally is critical for safety and must be prioritized alongside capability development.

resources.name: On the Responsible Scaling of AI
resources.title: On the Responsible Scaling of AI
resources.notes: Anthropic''s policy paper outlining its Responsible Scaling Policy, a framework for incrementally deploying more capable AI models while managing catastrophic risks through safety evaluations.
resources.author: Anthropic
resources.key_argument: Framework for when and how to scale AI safely, with commitments to pause if safety evaluations show dangerous capabilities.

resources.name: Superintelligence: Paths, Dangers, Strategies
resources.title: Superintelligence: Paths, Dangers, Strategies
resources.notes: A 2014 book by Oxford philosopher Nick Bostrom analyzing the potential trajectories, dangers, and strategic challenges posed by the development of machine superintelligence.
resources.author: Nick Bostrom
resources.key_argument: Foundational text arguing that superintelligent AI could pose existential risk if not aligned with human values.

resources.name: Platformer
resources.title: Platformer
resources.notes: A technology newsletter by journalist Casey Newton, published on Substack, covering the intersection of social media platforms, technology policy, and the tech industry.
resources.author: Casey Newton
resources.key_argument: Platformer covers technology policy and corporate accountability, including AI-related developments in content moderation and platform governance.

resources.name: AI Snake Oil
resources.title: AI Snake Oil
resources.notes: A book by Princeton researchers Arvind Narayanan and Sayash Kapoor critically examining exaggerated claims about AI capabilities and the harms caused by deploying unreliable AI systems.
resources.author: Arvind Narayanan, Sayash Kapoor
resources.key_argument: Critical perspective on AI hype, distinguishing genuine capabilities from marketing claims.

resources.name: Executive Order on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI
resources.title: Executive Order on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI
resources.notes: The formal title of President Biden''s October 2023 Executive Order (EO 14110) on artificial intelligence, establishing federal standards for AI safety, security, and trustworthiness.
resources.author: White House
resources.key_argument: Biden EO establishing safety standards, requiring reporting of large training runs, and directing agency action on AI.

resources.name: The Simple Macroeconomics of AI
resources.title: The Simple Macroeconomics of AI
resources.notes: An economics paper analyzing the macroeconomic implications of AI adoption, including effects on productivity, labor markets, and economic growth.
resources.author: Daron Acemoglu
resources.key_argument: AI will have modest economic effects in the near term, with GDP growth of only 0.5-1.5% over 10 years.

resources.name: Lex Fridman Podcast (AI episodes)
resources.title: Lex Fridman Podcast (AI episodes)
resources.notes: A long-form interview podcast hosted by Lex Fridman featuring in-depth conversations with AI researchers, policymakers, and technologists about artificial intelligence and its societal implications.
resources.author: Lex Fridman
resources.key_argument: Long-form interviews with AI leaders including Altman, Zuckerberg, Musk, Hassabis on capabilities and safety.

resources.name: International AI Safety Report
resources.title: International AI Safety Report
resources.notes: A 2024 international report led by Yoshua Bengio and a global panel of experts assessing the current state of AI safety risks and recommending governance approaches.
resources.author: UK AI Safety Institute
resources.key_argument: Expert consensus report on AI risks and safety measures, commissioned by the UK AI Safety Summit.

resources.name: Zvi Mowshowitz AI Newsletter
resources.title: Zvi Mowshowitz AI Newsletter
resources.notes: A weekly AI analysis newsletter by Zvi Mowshowitz (also known as ''Don''t Worry About the Vase'') providing detailed commentary on AI developments, policy, and safety research.
resources.author: Zvi Mowshowitz
resources.key_argument: Comprehensive weekly analysis of AI developments with focus on safety and existential risk.

resources.name: The Adolescence of Technology
resources.title: The Adolescence of Technology
resources.notes: Dario Amodei''s essay conceptualizing the current era of AI development as a ''technological adolescence'' — a volatile transitional period requiring deliberate governance and safety measures.
resources.author: Dario Amodei
resources.key_argument: AI development is in a critical adolescent phase where we must choose between responsible maturation and reckless acceleration.

resources.name: Life 3.0: Being Human in the Age of AI
resources.title: Life 3.0: Being Human in the Age of AI
resources.notes: A 2017 book by MIT physicist Max Tegmark exploring how artificial intelligence will transform life on Earth and examining the future trajectory of intelligent life in the universe.
resources.author: Max Tegmark
resources.key_argument: Humanity must proactively engage in AI safety research and develop wise governance strategies to ensure advanced AI remains beneficial and aligned with human values.

resources.name: Trump Attempts to Preempt State AI Regulation Through Executive Order
resources.title: Trump Attempts to Preempt State AI Regulation Through Executive Order
resources.notes: A news article reporting on President Trump''s executive order attempting to preempt state-level AI regulations in favor of a unified federal approach to AI governance.
resources.author: Ropes & Gray LLP
resources.key_argument: President Trump's executive order preempts state AI regulations by establishing federal agency actions and framework for future legislation.

resources.name: Trump signs executive order blocking states from enforcing their own regulations around AI | CNN Business
resources.title: Trump signs executive order blocking states from enforcing their own regulations around AI | CNN Business
resources.notes: A CNN Business article covering President Trump''s executive order aimed at blocking states from enforcing their own AI regulations, centralizing AI policy at the federal level.
resources.author: CNN Business
resources.key_argument: President Trump signed an executive order establishing federal preemption of state AI regulations to create a unified national framework for AI governance.

resources.name: Open Problems in Technical AI Governance
resources.title: Open Problems in Technical AI Governance
resources.notes: A research paper identifying and analyzing unresolved technical challenges in AI governance, including issues of compliance verification, auditing, and enforcement of AI regulations.
resources.author: Centre for the Governance of AI
resources.key_argument: Identifies key unsolved technical problems at the intersection of AI capabilities and governance, providing a research agenda for the field.

resources.name: A Collection of AI Governance Research Ideas
resources.title: A Collection of AI Governance Research Ideas
resources.notes: Research paper compiling a collection of open research questions and ideas in the field of AI governance, intended to guide future scholarship and policy work.
resources.author: GovAI
resources.key_argument: Curated database of research questions and project ideas for advancing AI governance.

resources.name: The Federal AI Preemption Push: President Trump signs the Executive Order titled “Ensuring a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence”
resources.title: The Federal AI Preemption Push: President Trump signs the Executive Order titled “Ensuring a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence”
resources.notes: Legal analysis article examining President Trump''s executive order on federal AI preemption, which aims to establish a unified national AI policy framework by overriding state-level AI regulations.
resources.author: Comm Law Group
resources.key_argument: Trump's executive order on a national AI policy framework asserts federal dominance over AI regulation by preempting state laws deemed inconsistent with national policy objectives.

resources.name: Highlights of the 2023 Executive Order on Artificial ...
resources.title: Highlights of the 2023 Executive Order on Artificial ...
resources.notes: Summary article highlighting the key provisions of President Biden''s 2023 executive order on artificial intelligence, which established new safety standards, equity protections, and innovation support measures for AI development.
resources.author: Congressional Research Service
resources.key_argument: This Congressional Research Service report summarizes the 2023 Executive Order establishing a comprehensive government-wide approach to ensure safe, secure, and trustworthy AI development and deployment.

resources.name: FACT SHEET: Biden-Harris Administration Executive Order Directs DHS to Lead the Responsible Development of Artificial Intelligence | Homeland Security
resources.title: FACT SHEET: Biden-Harris Administration Executive Order Directs DHS to Lead the Responsible Development of Artificial Intelligence | Homeland Security
resources.notes: Department of Homeland Security fact sheet detailing how the Biden-Harris Administration''s AI executive order directs DHS to lead responsible AI development, including safeguards for critical infrastructure and border security.
resources.author: U.S. Department of Homeland Security
resources.key_argument: The Biden-Harris Administration's executive order designates DHS as the lead agency for overseeing the responsible development and deployment of artificial intelligence across federal agencies.

resources.name: Biden signs U.S.' first AI executive order to create safeguards
resources.title: Biden signs U.S.' first AI executive order to create safeguards
resources.notes: News article covering President Biden''s signing of the first comprehensive U.S. executive order on artificial intelligence, which established safeguards around AI safety, security, and trustworthiness.
resources.author: CNBC
resources.key_argument: President Biden signed the U.S.'s first comprehensive AI executive order, establishing binding safety, equity, privacy, and security standards for AI development and deployment.

resources.name: Foundational Challenges in Assuring Alignment and Safety of Large Language Models
resources.title: Foundational Challenges in Assuring Alignment and Safety of Large Language Models
resources.notes: Academic paper examining the foundational technical and conceptual challenges in ensuring that large language models are aligned with human values and operate safely.
resources.author: Usman Anwar et al.
resources.key_argument: Comprehensive survey of fundamental open problems in LLM alignment, including specification, assurance, and societal impacts.

resources.name: Common Ground between AI 2027 & AI as Normal Technology
resources.title: Common Ground between AI 2027 & AI as Normal Technology
resources.notes: Essay exploring the points of agreement between the AI 2027 scenario (which envisions rapid, transformative AI progress) and the ''AI as Normal Technology'' perspective (which views AI as an incremental, manageable advance).
resources.author: AI Futures Project
resources.key_argument: Identifies shared assumptions between the accelerationist and cautious AI narratives, arguing the two camps agree on more than they think.

resources.name: Crisp and Fuzzy Tasks
resources.title: Crisp and Fuzzy Tasks
resources.notes: Essay distinguishing between ''crisp'' tasks (well-defined, easily measurable) and ''fuzzy'' tasks (ambiguous, judgment-dependent) in the context of AI capabilities and automation.
resources.author: Dwarkesh Patel
resources.key_argument: Distinguishes between tasks AI excels at (crisp, well-defined) vs tasks where human judgment remains essential (fuzzy, context-dependent).

resources.name: Alignment Remains a Hard, Unsolved Problem
resources.title: Alignment Remains a Hard, Unsolved Problem
resources.notes: Essay or paper arguing that AI alignment — ensuring AI systems reliably act in accordance with human intentions and values — remains a fundamentally difficult and unsolved problem despite recent progress.
resources.author: Paul Christiano
resources.key_argument: Argues that despite recent progress, the fundamental problem of ensuring AI systems reliably pursue human values remains unsolved.

resources.name: Computing Power and the Governance of Artificial Intelligence
resources.title: Computing Power and the Governance of Artificial Intelligence
resources.notes: Research paper analyzing the role of computing power as a governance lever for artificial intelligence, exploring how compute resources can be monitored, allocated, and regulated to shape AI development outcomes.
resources.author: Lennart Heim et al.
resources.key_argument: Compute is a key lever for AI governance because it is detectable, excludable, and quantifiable, making it uniquely suitable for regulation.

resources.name: US Senate Hearing On 'Examining the Harm of AI Chatbots'
resources.title: US Senate Hearing On 'Examining the Harm of AI Chatbots'
resources.notes: Official U.S. Senate hearing titled ''Examining the Harm of AI Chatbots,'' investigating the potential risks and harms posed by AI chatbot technologies to consumers and society.
resources.author: U.S. Senate
resources.key_argument: AI chatbots pose significant safety risks, particularly to children, as demonstrated by documented cases of self-harm and exploitation, necessitating congressional regulatory action.

resources.name: Transcript: US Senate Subcommittee Hearing on "Protecting Consumers from Artificial Intelligence Enabled Fraud and Scams" | TechPolicy.Press
resources.title: Transcript: US Senate Subcommittee Hearing on "Protecting Consumers from Artificial Intelligence Enabled Fraud and Scams" | TechPolicy.Press
resources.notes: Transcript of a U.S. Senate subcommittee hearing focused on protecting consumers from AI-enabled fraud and scams, published by TechPolicy.Press.
resources.author: US Senate Subcommittee
resources.key_argument: Congressional testimony addressing consumer protection from AI-enabled fraud through increased platform accountability, content authentication, and consumer education.

resources.name: White House Issues "One Rule" Executive Order To Curb State AI Regulation - New Technology - United States
resources.title: White House Issues "One Rule" Executive Order To Curb State AI Regulation - New Technology - United States
resources.notes: Legal analysis article (likely from Mondaq) examining the White House''s ''one rule'' executive order aimed at curbing state-level AI regulation through federal preemption. Covers similar ground to entities 661 and 681.
resources.author: White House
resources.key_argument: A White House executive order establishing a national AI regulation standard to preempt conflicting state laws and create uniform federal policy.

resources.name: Alignment Pretraining: AI Discourse Causes Self-Fulfilling (Mis)alignment
resources.title: Alignment Pretraining: AI Discourse Causes Self-Fulfilling (Mis)alignment
resources.notes: Research paper investigating how public discourse about AI alignment and safety can become self-fulfilling — shaping AI behavior during pretraining in ways that either help or hinder actual alignment outcomes.
resources.author: Geodesic Research
resources.key_argument: How AI models trained on alignment discourse may develop self-fulfilling beliefs about their own alignment or misalignment.

resources.name: Values in the Wild
resources.title: Values in the Wild
resources.notes: Research paper studying human values as they manifest ''in the wild'' — examining how values are embedded in, expressed through, and shaped by real-world AI systems and their deployment contexts.
resources.author: Anthropic
resources.key_argument: Empirical study of how Claude's values manifest in real-world usage, examining the gap between intended behavior and actual deployment.

resources.name: The Persona Selection Model: Why AI Assistants Might Behave Like Humans
resources.title: The Persona Selection Model: Why AI Assistants Might Behave Like Humans
resources.notes: Research paper proposing a ''persona selection model'' to explain why AI assistants may adopt human-like behavioral patterns, exploring the mechanisms by which LLMs select and enact particular personas.
resources.author: Samuel Marks, Alex Lindsey, Chris Olah
resources.key_argument: Proposes that LLMs select from a repertoire of learned personas rather than having a single fixed character, with implications for alignment.

resources.name: The Assistant Axis: Situating and Stabilizing the Character of LLMs
resources.title: The Assistant Axis: Situating and Stabilizing the Character of LLMs
resources.notes: Research paper on methods for situating and stabilizing the character or persona of large language models along an ''assistant axis,'' addressing consistency and reliability in LLM behavior.
resources.author: Samuel Marks et al.
resources.key_argument: Framework for understanding and stabilizing the character dimensions of language model assistants.

resources.name: Some Talent Needs in AI Governance
resources.title: Some Talent Needs in AI Governance
resources.notes: Essay identifying key talent gaps and workforce needs in the AI governance field, discussing what skills and roles are most urgently needed to support effective AI policy and oversight.
resources.author: Luke Muehlhauser
resources.key_argument: Identifies critical talent gaps in AI governance including technical policy expertise, international coordination, and compute governance.

resources.name: Toward A National AI Framework: The Federal Strategy To Override State Regulation - New Technology - United States
resources.title: Toward A National AI Framework: The Federal Strategy To Override State Regulation - New Technology - United States
resources.notes: Legal analysis article examining the federal strategy to establish a national AI framework that would override state-level regulation, covering the same Trump AI preemption executive order discussed in entities 661 and 674.
resources.author: Mondaq
resources.key_argument: The Trump Administration's Executive Order seeks to establish a federal AI framework that prioritizes national competitiveness while preempting state-level AI regulations.

resources.name: The Annual AI Governance Report 2025
resources.title: The Annual AI Governance Report 2025
resources.notes: Comprehensive annual report surveying the AI governance landscape in 2025, covering policy developments, institutional activities, and emerging trends in AI regulation and oversight worldwide.
resources.author: International Telecommunication Union (ITU)
resources.key_argument: Coordinated international efforts, standards, and ethical frameworks are essential for managing AI's global impact and ensuring responsible governance.

resources.name: The Annual AI Governance Report 2025: Steering the Future of AI
resources.title: The Annual AI Governance Report 2025: Steering the Future of AI
resources.notes: Annual report examining the state of AI governance in 2025, covering policy frameworks, regulatory developments, and strategies for steering AI''s future trajectory.
resources.author: World Economic Forum
resources.key_argument: Effective AI governance requires global, multi-stakeholder collaboration to develop inclusive and ethical frameworks that maximize AI's benefits while mitigating risks.

resources.name: AI Governance Profession Report 2025
resources.title: AI Governance Profession Report 2025
resources.notes: Report surveying the AI governance profession landscape in 2025, including roles, skills, and career trends for practitioners working in AI policy and oversight.
resources.author: International Association of Privacy Professionals (IAPP)
resources.key_argument: Organizations are increasingly establishing AI governance programs to ensure responsible and compliant AI deployment across their operations.

resources.name: AI Safety Index 2025: How Frontier AI Companies | Libertify
resources.title: AI Safety Index 2025: How Frontier AI Companies | Libertify
resources.notes: Coverage by Libertify of the Future of Life Institute''s 2025 AI Safety Index, which benchmarks how frontier AI companies manage safety practices.
resources.author: Libertify
resources.key_argument: The AI Safety Index 2025 benchmarks frontier AI companies on safety practices and risk management, showing significant variation in governance maturity.

resources.name: 2025 AI Safety Index - Future of Life Institute
resources.title: 2025 AI Safety Index - Future of Life Institute
resources.notes: The Future of Life Institute''s 2025 AI Safety Index, a benchmarking report assessing how leading AI companies address safety in their development practices.
resources.author: Future of Life Institute
resources.key_argument: While leading AI companies like Anthropic and OpenAI are advancing safety assessments and transparency measures, the broader AI industry lacks comprehensive safety standards and preparedness frameworks.

resources.name: AI Safety Index Winter 2025 - Future of Life Institute
resources.title: AI Safety Index Winter 2025 - Future of Life Institute
resources.notes: The Winter 2025 edition of the Future of Life Institute''s AI Safety Index, evaluating frontier AI companies'' safety commitments and practices.
resources.author: Future of Life Institute
resources.key_argument: The Winter 2025 AI Safety Index reveals substantial variations in safety practices among leading AI companies, with Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google demonstrating stronger safety governance than industry peers.

resources.name: Canaries in the Coal Mine? Six Facts about the Recent Employment Effects of Artificial Intelligence
resources.title: Canaries in the Coal Mine? Six Facts about the Recent Employment Effects of Artificial Intelligence
resources.notes: Economics research paper presenting six key findings on the early employment effects of artificial intelligence, framing affected workers as ''canaries in the coal mine'' for broader labor market disruption.
resources.author: Erik Brynjolfsson, Danielle Li, Lindsey Raymond, Tom Sheehy
resources.key_argument: Analysis of recent AI employment effects shows generative AI adoption has disproportionately impacted entry-level workers aged 22-25 in AI-exposed occupations, with measurable wage and employment declines.

resources.name: MIT study finds AI can already replace 11.7% of U.S. ...
resources.title: MIT study finds AI can already replace 11.7% of U.S. ...
resources.notes: News article covering an MIT study finding that AI technology is already capable of replacing approximately 11.7% of tasks in the U.S. workforce.
resources.author: CNBC
resources.key_argument: An MIT study estimates that AI can currently replace approximately 11.7% of the U.S. workforce (roughly 18 million workers), representing approximately $1.2 trillion in annual wage exposure.

resources.name: Transcript: US House Subcommittee Hosts Hearing on "AI ...
resources.title: Transcript: US House Subcommittee Hosts Hearing on "AI ...
resources.notes: Transcript of a U.S. House subcommittee hearing examining artificial intelligence policy, published by TechPolicy.Press.
resources.author: US House Subcommittee
resources.key_argument: Congressional debate on AI regulation reflected partisan divisions, with Democrats prioritizing consumer protections while Republicans opposed proposed moratoriums on AI development.

resources.name: Adam Thierer Testimony before JEC on AI & Economic Opportunity
resources.title: Adam Thierer Testimony before JEC on AI & Economic Opportunity
resources.notes: Written testimony by Adam Thierer before the Joint Economic Committee on the economic opportunities presented by AI and the policy conditions needed to realize them.
resources.author: Adam Thierer
resources.key_argument: AI presents substantial opportunities for economic growth and productivity gains across sectors, though realizing these benefits requires appropriate policy frameworks that avoid over-regulation.

resources.name: Senate Judiciary written testimony - Helen Toner 2024-09-17
resources.title: Senate Judiciary written testimony - Helen Toner 2024-09-17
resources.notes: Written testimony by Helen Toner, former OpenAI board member, before the Senate Judiciary Committee in September 2024, offering an insider''s perspective on AI governance and safety.
resources.author: Helen Toner
resources.key_argument: AI companies developing toward artificial general intelligence view AGI as a serious and imminent goal, raising urgent policy questions about oversight and safety measures needed before advanced AI systems are deployed.

resources.name: Machines of Loving Grace
resources.title: Machines of Loving Grace
resources.notes: Essay by Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei (October 2024) exploring the potential positive impacts of advanced AI on science, health, economic development, and governance.
resources.author: Dario Amodei
resources.key_argument: AI development offers transformative benefits like disease curing and poverty reduction, but realizing this optimistic vision requires deliberate focus on safety and alignment alongside capability advances.

resources.name: The Federal AI Preemption Push: President Trump Signs The Executive Order Titled "Ensuring A National Policy Framework For Artificial Intelligence" - New Technology - United States
resources.title: The Federal AI Preemption Push: President Trump Signs The Executive Order Titled "Ensuring A National Policy Framework For Artificial Intelligence" - New Technology - United States
resources.notes: Legal analysis published on Mondaq examining President Trump''s executive order on ensuring a national AI policy framework, focusing on its implications for federal preemption of state AI regulations.
resources.author: Mondaq
resources.key_argument: President Trump signed an executive order establishing a national AI policy framework designed to preempt state-level AI regulations.

resources.name: Transcript: Senate Judiciary Subcommittee Hosts Hearing on Oversight of AI: Insiders’ Perspectives | TechPolicy.Press
resources.title: Transcript: Senate Judiciary Subcommittee Hosts Hearing on Oversight of AI: Insiders’ Perspectives | TechPolicy.Press
resources.notes: Transcript of a Senate Judiciary Subcommittee hearing featuring insider perspectives on AI oversight, published by TechPolicy.Press.
resources.author: Senate Judiciary Subcommittee
resources.key_argument: Industry insiders testify before the Senate Judiciary Subcommittee on the necessity for enforceable AI regulations and corporate accountability measures to balance innovation with safety and public interest.

resources.name: Blackburn Unveils National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence - U.S. S...
resources.title: Blackburn Unveils National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence - U.S. S...
resources.notes: Announcement by Senator Marsha Blackburn of a national policy framework for artificial intelligence, outlining proposed federal AI governance principles.
resources.author: Marsha Blackburn
resources.key_argument: Senator Blackburn's legislative proposal for the TRUMP AMERICA AI Act, establishing a unified federal framework for AI policy with protections for children and consumers.

resources.name: 2025 Q4 Update from our Frontier AI Risk Monitoring Platform
resources.title: 2025 Q4 Update from our Frontier AI Risk Monitoring Platform
resources.notes: Quarterly update (Q4 2025) from a frontier AI risk monitoring platform, reporting on emerging risks and safety developments at leading AI labs.
resources.author: AI Safety China
resources.key_argument: AI Safety China's Q4 2025 frontier AI monitoring platform reports stabilized overall risk levels across frontier models, though certain model families continue to present elevated risks requiring targeted mitigation.

resources.name: Labor Demand in the Age of Generative AI : Early Evidence from the U.S. Job Posting Data
resources.title: Labor Demand in the Age of Generative AI : Early Evidence from the U.S. Job Posting Data
resources.notes: Economics research paper analyzing how generative AI is affecting U.S. labor demand, using job posting data to identify early shifts in employer hiring patterns.
resources.author: David Autor, Simon Jäger, David Sands, Pascaul Restrepo
resources.key_argument: U.S. job posting data reveals that generative AI tools like ChatGPT have led to significant declines in labor demand for certain occupational categories.

resources.name: Transcript: Joint Hearing on “Artificial Intelligence and Its Potential to Fuel Economic Growth and Improve Governance” | TechPolicy.Press
resources.title: Transcript: Joint Hearing on “Artificial Intelligence and Its Potential to Fuel Economic Growth and Improve Governance” | TechPolicy.Press
resources.notes: Transcript of a joint congressional hearing on artificial intelligence''s potential to fuel economic growth and improve governance, published by TechPolicy.Press.
resources.author: U.S. Congress
resources.key_argument: A congressional joint hearing examining artificial intelligence's potential to drive economic growth and improve governance through expert testimony on AI applications.
